# Findings: Claude settings overlay, package install, and notifications

## 1. The Claude settings-overlay merger

**File path:** `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl`

**Gating / templating:** The entire body is wrapped in `{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}` … `{{ end -}}`. The overlay JSON is embedded at render time via `{{ include "claude/settings-overlay.json" }}` into a PowerShell here-string, so the script is self-contained and chezmoi re-fires it whenever the overlay content changes (the `run_onchange` mechanism hashes the rendered script). It respects `$env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` (falling back to `~/.claude`), targets `settings.json`, and uses `$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'` (best-effort, never aborts the apply).

**Merge logic — this is the critical part for your plan. It is a recursive OBJECT deep-merge, but arrays are REPLACED wholesale, NOT appended.** It is NOT hook-aware. Here is the exact merge function:

```powershell
$overlay = $overlayJson | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable

# Recursive deep-merge: overlay leaves win; live-only keys are preserved.
function Merge-Hashtable([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
    foreach ($k in $overlay.Keys) {
        if ($base.ContainsKey($k) -and ($base[$k] -is [hashtable]) -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
            Merge-Hashtable $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
        } else {
            $base[$k] = $overlay[$k]
        }
    }
}
```

Behavior: recursion happens **only when both sides are hashtables (JSON objects)**. For any other type — including arrays — it takes the `else` branch: `$base[$k] = $overlay[$k]`, a wholesale replacement.

**What this means for notification hooks (important gotcha):** In Claude Code's `settings.json`, `hooks` is an object keyed by event name (`"Notification"`, `"Stop"`, `"PreToolUse"`, …), and each event's value is an **array** of matcher/hook entries. With this merger:
- `hooks` itself is a hashtable → the merge recurses into it (good — other event keys the user has live are preserved).
- But `hooks.Notification` is an **array** → the `else` branch fires and the overlay's `hooks.Notification` array **replaces** any live `hooks.Notification` array entirely. It does NOT append entries not already present.

So if you add a `hooks.Notification` entry via the overlay and the user later adds their own `Notification` hook at runtime, the next `chezmoi apply` would clobber the user's. Conversely, since the overlay is the only managed source, clobbering the overlay's own array is fine. If you need additive/append-if-absent array semantics (like the parent repo's jq merge), you must extend `Merge-Hashtable` to special-case arrays (e.g., union by serialized-entry identity). This is explicitly called out as a known limitation — the parent repo (macOS/Linux `dot_claude/modify_settings.json`) uses a jq additive-array hook merge that was deliberately NOT ported here because the current overlay has no `hooks` key (confirmed in `.claude/plans/snazzy-painting-wilkes.md` and the specstory transcript at `.specstory/history/2026-07-12_07-48-49Z-at-users-david-local.md`: "did not port the jq additive-array hook merge — the chosen overlay has no `hooks` key… If you add hooks to the overlay later, that needs revisiting.").

Rest of the script (path resolution + write):
```powershell
$claudeDir = if ($env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) { $env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR } else { Join-Path $HOME '.claude' }
$settingsPath = Join-Path $claudeDir 'settings.json'
$settings = @{}
if (Test-Path $settingsPath) {
    $raw = Get-Content -Raw $settingsPath
    if ($raw.Trim()) {
        try { $settings = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable }
        catch { Write-Warning "could not parse $settingsPath; not overwriting"; return }
    }
}
Merge-Hashtable $settings $overlay
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $claudeDir | Out-Null
$settings | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 12 | Set-Content -Path $settingsPath -Encoding utf8
```
Note `-Depth 12` on `ConvertTo-Json` — deep hook structures are within that depth, but worth remembering.

## 3. Package installation

**File path:** `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` (this is described in-file as "the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for what gets installed").

**Fault-tolerance pattern:** `$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'`, a `$script:Failures` list, and `Register-Failure($what)` which records + warns but never throws. At the end it prints a summary of all skipped failures. Helper `Have($n)` = `[bool](Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)`.

**Representative install patterns (each wired to Register-Failure):**

- **scoop** — batch install of only-missing apps; re-checks after and registers any still-absent:
```powershell
function Scoop-Install([string[]]$apps) {
    $missing = @($apps | Where-Object { ($_ -split '/')[-1] -notin (Get-ScoopInstalled) })
    if (-not $missing) { Info 'scoop: all requested tools already present'; return }
    try { scoop install @missing } catch { Write-Warning "scoop batch error: $_" }
    $after = Get-ScoopInstalled
    foreach ($app in $missing) {
        $name = ($app -split '/')[-1]
        if ($name -notin $after) { Register-Failure "scoop:$name" }
    }
}
```
Buckets added by `Ensure-Scoop`: `main`, `extras`, `versions`, `nerd-fonts`.

- **winget** — idempotent (skip if `winget list` succeeds), user scope then machine-scope retry, else register:
```powershell
function Winget-Install([string]$id) {
    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "winget:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }
    winget install --id $id -e --silent --scope user --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { winget install --id $id -e --silent --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host }
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "winget:$id" }
}
```
There is also `Winget-InstallStore` for `--source msstore` product-id installs (Raycast, ChatGPT).

- **npm global** (used only for coding agents):
```powershell
function Npm-InstallGlobal([string[]]$pkgs) {
    if (-not (Have npm)) { foreach ($p in $pkgs) { Register-Failure "npm:$p (npm unavailable)" }; return }
    foreach ($p in $pkgs) {
        npm install -g $p 2>&1 | Out-Host
        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "npm:$p" }
    }
}
```

- **PSGallery / Install-Module** — non-interactive prep (NuGet provider + trust PSGallery) then per-module guarded `Install-Module … -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense`:
```powershell
if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name PSFzf)) {
    Install-Module PSFzf -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
}
```
(Same shape for `AudioDeviceCmdlets`.)

**Python / pip / pipx / uv — YES, `uv` is the Python mechanism (this is your apprise install path):**
- `uv` is installed via **scoop** in the always-installed core toolset (line 164: `'uv', 'gnupg',`).
- Python itself is installed **by uv**, not scoop/winget (lines 211–216):
```powershell
if (Have uv) {
    $env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
    uv python install --default --preview 2>&1 | Out-Host
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:python' }
}
```
- There is **no pip / pipx** anywhere. The established pattern for installing a Python CLI tool is **`uv tool install`**, already used for LiteLLM under `installLlmTools` (lines 335–337) — this is the exact template you'd copy for apprise:
```powershell
if (Have uv) { Info 'uv tool install litellm'; uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:litellm' } }
```
So `apprise` would install as `uv tool install apprise` (guarded by `if (Have uv)`), which drops the `apprise` executable into `~/.local/bin` (`UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR` / uv tool bin dir, already on PATH per `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1`). Note: apprise's Windows backend (`windows://`) needs the `pywin32` package; `uv tool install apprise` alone may not pull it — you may need `uv tool install --with pywin32 apprise` (worth verifying against apprise's Windows service docs at appriseit.com/services/windows).

**Toggle → install gating map** (from the template `{{ if .X }}` blocks):
| Toggle | Installs |
|---|---|
| (always) | core CLI incl. `uv`, `nodejs-lts`, `bun`, `gcc`, `tree-sitter`; uv Python; PSFzf; AudioDeviceCmdlets; gh-dash (if gh authed) |
| `installCodingAgents` | Claude Code (winget `Anthropic.ClaudeCode`), npm `opencode-ai`/`@openai/codex`/`@github/copilot`, scoop `opencode-desktop` — **also gates the settings-overlay merger (§1)** |
| `installExtraRuntimes` | scoop `go`, `rustup`, `ruby` |
| `installMediaTools` | scoop `ffmpeg`, `imagemagick` |
| `installWindowsApps` | GUI apps + editors + Nerd Fonts via winget/msstore |
| `installGamingApps` | winget `Valve.Steam` |
| `installUtilityApps` | CPU-Z, GPU-Z, HWiNFO, TreeSize, Everything, ShareX, VLC, OBS, Rufus, (Tailscale unless managed) |
| `installLlmTools` | winget `Ollama.Ollama` + **`uv tool install litellm[proxy]`** |
| `installTunnelTools` | scoop `ngrok`, `cloudflared` |
| `installIacTools` | scoop `terraform`, `opentofu`; winget Azure CLI; `az extension add azure-devops` |
| `installHerdr` | `Install-Herdr` (irm\|iex bootstrap) |
| `installClink` | scoop `clink` + Lua plugin fetches |
| `installTry` | scoop `ruby` + `gem install try-cli` |
| `useChineseMirror` | sets `PIP_INDEX_URL`, `UV_DEFAULT_INDEX`, npm/go/node mirror env vars at top of script |
| `managedMachine` | *excludes* Grammarly / Tailscale |

## 4. Init prompts in `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl`

**File path:** `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.chezmoi.toml.tmpl`. The `[data]` block uses helper funcs `promptBoolOnce`, `promptStringOnce`, `promptChoiceOnce` (the `Once` variants persist answers to `%USERPROFILE%\.config\chezmoi\chezmoi.toml` and never re-ask). `$full := eq $role "workstation"` is the common default for "full desktop" toggles.

Full list of `installX` / toggle prompts (name + exact prompt text + default):

- `role` (choice `workstation|minimal`, default `workstation`) — "Role: workstation (full desktop) or minimal (shell only)"
- `name` (string, "Da-Wei Lee") — "Your full name (git)"
- `email` (string) — "Your git email"
- `installCodingAgents` (bool, default `$full`) — "Install coding agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Copilot CLI, SpecStory)"
- `installWindowsApps` (bool, `$full`) — "Install GUI apps via winget (VSCode, Cursor, Notepad++, Terminal, Alacritty, PowerToys, Raycast)"
- `installWsl` (bool, `$full`) — "Install WSL2 (Docker Desktop backend; needs admin + reboot)"
- `installWslUbuntu` (bool, `false`) — "Install a WSL2 Ubuntu distro and bootstrap cross-platform dotfiles inside it (needs admin; requires WSL2 platform)"
- `wslUsername` (string, `.chezmoi.username | lower`) — "WSL Ubuntu username"
- `wslUbuntuBootstrap` (choice `headless|interactive|none`, `headless`) — "WSL Ubuntu dotfiles bootstrap (headless|interactive|none)"
- `installUtilityApps` (bool, `$full`) — "Install utility apps via winget (CPU-Z, GPU-Z, TreeSize, VLC, Everything, ShareX, HWiNFO)"
- `installGamingApps` (bool, `false`) — "Install gaming apps (Steam)"
- `installExtraRuntimes` (bool, `$full`) — "Install extra runtimes (rust, go, ruby) beyond node/bun/uv"
- `installMediaTools` (bool, `false`) — "Install media CLIs (ffmpeg, imagemagick)"
- `installLlmTools` (bool, `false`) — "Install local LLM tools (Ollama, LiteLLM)"
- `installTunnelTools` (bool, `false`) — "Install tunnel tools (ngrok, cloudflared)"
- `installIacTools` (bool, `false`) — "Install Infrastructure-as-Code tools (Azure CLI, Terraform, OpenTofu)"
- `installSshServer` (bool, `false`) — "Install and enable the OpenSSH server / sshd (needs admin, opens inbound port 22)"
- `installHerdr` (bool, `false`) — "Install herdr (native Windows terminal multiplexer, preview beta)"
- `installClink` (bool, `false`) — "Install Clink + starship/zoxide/fzf for cmd.exe (DOS prompt)"
- `installTry` (bool, `false`) — "Install try (Ruby ephemeral-workspace CLI, needs Ruby)"
- `useChineseMirror` (bool, `false`) — "Use China (GFW) mirrors for pip/npm/cargo/go/node"
- `managedMachine` (bool, `false`) — "Managed or corporate machine (skip org-policy-blocked apps like Tailscale and Grammarly)"
- `backupMode` (choice `smart|full|off`, `smart`) — "Back up existing files before apply (smart|full|off)"
- `enableVimMode` (bool, `true`) — "Enable PSReadLine vi editing mode"

**Pattern for adding `installApprise`:** copy one `promptBoolOnce` line, e.g.
`installApprise = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installApprise" "Install apprise for desktop notifications (uv tool)" false }}`
Then reference `.installApprise` in the packages template and/or gate the overlay-hooks addition. **However**, given apprise notifications are only meaningful with Claude Code (whose hooks fire them) and the overlay merger is already gated on `installCodingAgents`, the simplest design is to **piggyback on `installCodingAgents`** rather than add a new toggle — install `apprise` via `uv tool install` inside the existing `{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}` block in the packages script, and add the `hooks` key to `settings-overlay.json` (also `installCodingAgents`-gated). A separate `installApprise` toggle only earns its keep if you want notifications independent of coding agents. Note also that the toggle must be added to the `.installLlmTools`-style docs list AND (for clarity) to the comment header block at the top of the packages template (lines 7–22) which mirrors every toggle.

## 5. Existing notification mechanism

**None exists in this Windows repo.** Confirmed by grepping tracked files (excluding `.git`, worktrees, and `.specstory/history/` transcripts):
- No `BurntToast`, `SnoreToast`, `New-BurntToastNotification`, `msg.exe`, `toast`, `windows://`, or `apprise` in any `.ps1`, `.tmpl`, `.json`, `.yaml`, or `.md`.
- No `dot_config/apprise/` directory and no `apprise.yaml.tmpl` (the apprise config seen in `.specstory/history/2026-07-09_13-02-08Z-continue.md` belongs to the **separate cross-platform repo** at `~/.local/share/chezmoi`, not this Windows repo — that transcript references `dot_config/apprise/apprise.yaml.tmpl` with a `windows://` branch as prior art you can mirror).
- The only `notify` hits are in SpecStory transcripts and the packages-script comment about `gh-notify` (a bash-only gh extension intentionally skipped on Windows) — unrelated to desktop notifications.
- Profile fragments in `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/` (`00_env.ps1` … `98_tv_cache.ps1`) contain no notification helpers. `AudioDeviceCmdlets` (installed via PSGallery) is for volume/mute, not notifications.

**So you are building this from scratch.** The pieces you'd assemble:
1. `uv tool install apprise` (likely `--with pywin32`) — add to packages template, `installCodingAgents`-gated (or new `installApprise`), following the LiteLLM `uv tool install` + `Register-Failure` precedent.
2. A `hooks` key in `claude/settings-overlay.json` for the `Notification` (and/or `Stop`) events invoking apprise. Because the merger REPLACES arrays wholesale, either accept overlay-owns-the-array semantics, or upgrade `Merge-Hashtable` to do append-if-absent for array values (mirroring the parent repo's jq additive merge). The parent repo's `dot_claude/modify_settings.json` at `~/.local/share/chezmoi` is the reference implementation for the additive hook-merge and the apprise-notify command shape.
3. Optionally an `apprise.yaml` config with a `windows://` service, mirroring the cross-platform repo's `dot_config/apprise/apprise.yaml.tmpl`.

Key files to touch: `claude/settings-overlay.json`, `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`, and (if adding a toggle) `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` + the toggle-mirror comment block at the top of the packages template. The merger script `run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl` only needs editing if you want additive array semantics.

# How the unix/macOS dotfiles wire up Claude Code desktop notifications

The short version: **the notify script does NOT do any platform detection or backend selection itself — it unconditionally calls `apprise --tag desktop`, and all platform logic lives in the chezmoi-templated `apprise.yaml`.** And crucially for you: **the `apprise.yaml.tmpl` already has a `windows://` branch.** This repo's design is exactly the pattern you want to mirror.

## 1. The notify hook script

**File:** `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_claude/hooks/executable_notify.sh.tmpl`
- Chezmoi renders this to `~/.claude/hooks/notify.sh` (the `executable_` prefix sets +x; the `.tmpl` suffix means it's templated — though this particular file contains no template directives, so it's byte-identical on every OS).

Full file (29 lines):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Claude Code notification hook using apprise
# Notifications are configured in ~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml

# Read JSON from stdin
json="$(cat)"

# Parse fields
event="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.hook_event_name // "unknown"')"
title="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.title // "Claude Code"')"
msg="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.message // empty')"
ntype="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.notification_type // empty')"

# Handle Stop event (no message/title in payload)
if [[ "$event" == "Stop" ]]; then
  title="Claude Code"
  msg="Task finished"
fi

# Build full title with notification type
full_title="$title"
[[ -n "$ntype" ]] && full_title="$title [$ntype]"

# Send notification via apprise using desktop tag
# Desktop notifications are auto-detected by OS in apprise.yaml
apprise --tag desktop --title "$full_title" --body "$msg" 2>/dev/null || true
```

Answers to your specific questions:

- **Backend(s):** Exactly one — **`apprise`**. No terminal-notifier, no osascript, no notify-send, no ntfy, no direct anything. Every OS goes through the single `apprise` CLI call. It relies on `jq` for parsing (installed by the `base` ansible role).
- **Does it use apprise?** Yes, and it is the *only* backend. The invocation is:
  ```
  apprise --tag desktop --title "$full_title" --body "$msg"
  ```
  It selects the target by the **`--tag desktop`** filter, not by a URL on the command line. The actual URL/service is defined in the config file `~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml` (see section 2). The `2>/dev/null || true` makes the hook non-fatal if apprise is missing or fails.
- **How it decides platform (macOS vs Linux vs Windows):** It does **not**. There is zero OS-detection logic in the script. Platform selection is fully delegated to apprise's config file, which is chezmoi-templated per-OS. This is the clean separation you'll want to copy: the hook is OS-agnostic; the config picks the backend.
- **What it reads from Claude Code's hook stdin payload:** It `cat`s the JSON from stdin and pulls four fields with `jq`:
  - `.hook_event_name` → `event` (default `"unknown"`)
  - `.title` → `title` (default `"Claude Code"`)
  - `.message` → `msg` (default empty)
  - `.notification_type` → `ntype` (default empty)
  
  Special case: when `event == "Stop"` the payload carries no title/message, so it hard-codes `title="Claude Code"` and `msg="Task finished"`. The final notification title becomes `"$title [$ntype]"` when a notification_type is present, else just `"$title"`. (Note: it does **not** read session id, cwd, transcript_path, or any other fields Claude Code sends.)

## 2. Apprise: install + config (all hits)

### How apprise is INSTALLED

**File:** `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_ansible/roles/python_uv_tools/defaults/main.yml` (lines 34-35):

```yaml
  - name: apprise
    binary: apprise
```

It is installed as a **`uv tool install apprise`** (a global uv tool, exposing the `apprise` binary in `~/.local/bin`). Not pipx, not brew, not a plain pip. The `python_uv_tools` ansible role iterates this list and runs `uv tool install` for each entry, using the `binary:` name for its idempotency `creates:` guard. Confirmed by the tool-manager docs:
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/this_repo/tool-managers.md:974`: `| **apprise** | uv tool | uv tool | python_uv_tools |`
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/this_repo/architecture.md:119`: lists apprise under the `python_uv_tools` role.
- `README.md:320`: "Python tools (via uv): thefuck, apprise, sqlit-tui, …"
- Upgrades ride along with `just upgrade-uv` → `uv tool upgrade --all` (README:364).

For Windows you'd instead do `uv tool install apprise` (or `pipx install apprise`) — same CLI, and you'd want the `windows://` extra which pulls in `win10toast` on Windows.

### How apprise is CONFIGURED

The config lives in `~/.config/apprise/` (rendered from `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/`). Three source files:

**a) `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/apprise.yaml.tmpl`** — the main config, templated per-OS. This is the heart of the platform selection and **it already contains your `windows://` case**:

```yaml
version: 1

# Include user's custom notification services
# custom.yaml is created once by chezmoi and never overwritten
# Edit it to add your own services (Discord, Slack, Telegram, etc.)
include:
  - custom.yaml

# Default system-wide desktop notifications (auto-detected by OS)
urls:
{{- if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" }}
  # macOS native notifications
  - macosx://:
      tag: desktop
{{- else if eq .chezmoi.os "linux" }}
  # Linux DBus notifications (works with most DEs)
  - dbus://:
      tag: desktop
{{- else if eq .chezmoi.os "windows" }}
  # Windows native notifications
  - windows://:
      tag: desktop
{{- end }}
```

Key mechanics to mirror:
- The `tag: desktop` on the URL is what the hook's `--tag desktop` matches against. That's the entire wiring between hook and backend.
- Per-OS `{{ if eq .chezmoi.os ... }}` selects `macosx://` / `dbus://` / `windows://`. The `windows://` URL is the standard apprise Windows toast backend (uses `win10toast` under the hood).
- It `include:`s a sibling `custom.yaml` for user-added remote services.

**b) `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/create_custom.yaml`** — the `create_` prefix means chezmoi writes it **once and never overwrites** (this is the mechanism that keeps user secrets safe, used *instead of* a `.chezmoiignore` entry — I checked `.chezmoiignore*` and custom.yaml is not listed there). Renders to `~/.config/apprise/custom.yaml`:

```yaml
# Your custom Apprise notification services
# This file is created once and never overwritten by chezmoi
# See example.yaml for configuration examples
version: 1
urls:
  # - discord://webhook_id/webhook_token:
  #     tag: chat
  []
```

**c) `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/example.yaml`** — a tracked, committed reference file (safe, no secrets) showing Discord/Slack/mailto/Telegram/Pushover/json URL examples with `tag:` groupings. Points at `https://appriseit.com/getting-started/configuration/`.

### Other apprise references (non-code)

- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/backlog/audit-monitor-with-apprise.md` — a design/research doc (NOT implemented) for a future `audit-monitor` helper that would reuse apprise for scheduled sysadmin alerts. Most relevant part for you is its **secret-management discussion (section C)**: it explicitly warns apprise URLs contain auth tokens and must not be synced via dotfiles; documents three patterns (Bitwarden CLI fetch / per-host `0600` env file in `.chezmoiignore` / systemd `LoadCredentialEncrypted=`). This is why `custom.yaml` uses the create-once pattern.
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/TODO.md:89` — the backlog item's index entry.
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/tools/agent-overlays.md:293` — documents a *future* idea to extend `notify.sh` to also fire `apprise --tag webhook` for remote chat delivery, gated behind an env var `CLAUDE_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK=1`. Not implemented, but shows the intended extension model (add more `tag:`-grouped URLs to `custom.yaml`, add a second `apprise --tag webhook` call to the hook).
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/backlog/tmux-window-status-indicators.md:78` — mentions reusing "the existing apprise call" for tmux status.
- Docs tables at `docs/this_repo/tool-managers.md` and `architecture.md` (+ their `.zh-TW` translations) — just catalog apprise as a uv tool.
- (The `.venv/` and `.claude/worktrees/` hits are noise — a vendored watchdog lib and duplicate worktree copies of the same files.)

## 3. The `modify_settings.json` overlay — hook events wired

**File:** `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_claude/modify_settings.json` (a chezmoi `modify_` script that hook-aware deep-merges an overlay into `~/.claude/settings.json`). Confirmed the events and commands it enforces:

| Event | Command(s) it appends |
|---|---|
| **`Notification`** | (1) `~/.claude/hooks/notify.sh` — the apprise notify. (2) `command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status waiting \|\| true` |
| **`UserPromptSubmit`** | `command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status working \|\| true` |
| **`Stop`** | (1) `~/.claude/hooks/notify.sh` — the apprise notify. (2) `command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status done \|\| true` |
| **`SubagentStop`** | `command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status done \|\| true` |

So `notify.sh` is fired on exactly two events: **`Notification`** and **`Stop`**. (SubagentStop and UserPromptSubmit only drive workmux window-status, not notifications.)

Important detail about *how* it merges (relevant if you replicate the overlay approach on Windows): it does an **additive, hook-aware merge** — for `.hooks.<event>` arrays it appends only overlay entries whose `.hooks[0].command` isn't already present, preserving entries that CodeIsland / workmux `wm setup` inject at runtime. A naive `jq '. * $overlay'` would wipe those out on every `chezmoi apply`. It also has a jq-not-yet-installed cold-start fallback (passes the live file through unchanged). The overlay additionally sets `enabledPlugins`, `extraKnownMarketplaces` (claude-hud), `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt`, `permissions.defaultMode: auto`, and a `statusLine` command.

## 4. Env vars / config paths the notify flow depends on

There are **no notification env vars** in the current implementation — I grepped for `NTFY_TOPIC`, `APPRISE`, `NTFY`, etc. in the hooks dir and `dot_zshrc*` and found nothing wired up. The design deliberately keeps zero env-var coupling. Specifically:

- **`~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml`** — the only path the hook implicitly depends on (apprise's default config search path; the comment in notify.sh names it explicitly). Contains the per-OS `--tag desktop` URL.
- **`~/.config/apprise/custom.yaml`** — included by apprise.yaml; where a user adds remote services. Create-once, never synced-over.
- **`jq`** on PATH — required by the hook to parse stdin (installed by the `base` ansible role).
- **`apprise`** on PATH (in `~/.local/bin` via uv tool) — the actual sender.
- Env vars that appear only in *unimplemented* proposals: `CLAUDE_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK=1` (docs/tools/agent-overlays.md:293), and `APPRISE_URL_OPS` / `APPRISE_URL_SECURITY` (backlog/audit-monitor). Neither is active today.

## What this means for your Windows mirror

The pattern is remarkably clean to replicate — the repo already did 90% of the Windows work:

1. Install apprise on Windows (`uv tool install apprise` or `pipx install apprise`). Ensure the Windows toast dependency is present (apprise's `windows://` uses `win10toast`).
2. Ship an OS-agnostic `notify.sh`/`notify.ps1` equivalent that just calls `apprise --tag desktop --title ... --body ...` reading the Claude hook JSON from stdin (fields `.hook_event_name`, `.title`, `.message`, `.notification_type`; Stop event → "Task finished").
3. Provide `~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml` with:
   ```yaml
   - windows://:
       tag: desktop
   ```
   — which is literally the `{{ else if eq .chezmoi.os "windows" }}` branch already present in `apprise.yaml.tmpl`.
4. Wire the Claude `Notification` and `Stop` hook events to run the script (mirror of `modify_settings.json`), ideally with the same additive-merge care if other tools also write to `~/.claude/settings.json`.
5. Keep the remote-service URLs (which carry secrets) in a create-once, non-synced `custom.yaml`, per the backlog doc's secret-management guidance.

The single most useful source file to copy is `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/apprise.yaml.tmpl` — it's already cross-platform and includes the `windows://` case.

.specstory/history/2026-07-10_06-25-00Z-at-users-david-local.md:2438:I need to look at the actual `28_tldr.zsh` implementation to properly port `tldrf`, and I should also understand what tldr client is being used (looks like tlrc, the Rust implementation). I also need to check what gh extensions are installed in the parent configuration—specifically `dlvhdr/gh-dash`, `meiji163/gh-notify`, and `remcostoeten/gh-select`, which are set up through some installation mechanism. I need to check whether the Windows repo already has tldr and gh extensions installed, and figure out where to add the tldrf function—probably as a new profile fragment.
.specstory/history/2026-07-10_06-25-00Z-at-users-david-local.md:2468:`echo "=== Windows repo: tldr / tlrc / gh extension already present? ===" && grep -rniE 'tldr|tlrc|gh extension|gh-dash|gh-notify|gh-select' .chezmoiscripts/ dot_config/powershell/ docs/ 2>/dev/null | head -20; echo "=== profile.d numbering (where tldrf fragment would go) ==="; ls dot_config/powershell/profile.d/`
.specstory/history/2026-07-10_06-25-00Z-at-users-david-local.md:2542:Now for the gh extensions—the parent installs a few via `gh extension install` when gh is authenticated, and that same command works on Windows too.  `dlvhdr/gh-dash` is a Go binary so it's cross-platform and fine. But `meiji163/gh-notify` is a bash script extension that relies on fzf, less, and python, which won't work natively on Windows without a bash environment. That's going to be a compatibility issue I need to think through.
.specstory/history/2026-07-10_06-25-00Z-at-users-david-local.md:2546:So I should report that the Windows repo currently has no gh extensions, note what the parent repo has (gh-dash, gh-notify, gh-select), and flag the Windows compatibility issue with the shell-script ones before suggesting whether to port them over.
.specstory/history/2026-07-10_06-25-00Z-at-users-david-local.md:2550:Looking at the compatibility angle: gh-dash is a Go binary and works fine on Windows, but gh-notify and gh-select are bash scripts that won't run natively in PowerShell. So the practical approach is to implement tldr, tldrf, and gh-dash (all Windows-safe), then flag the shell-script extensions as incompatible and let the user decide if they want to handle them differently. ask if they want gh-dash added too, since it's Windows-compatible, while flagging that gh-notify and gh-select are bash-only and won't work on Windows. But given the user's pattern of saying "sure, add both," I'm going to make a call: implement the clearly safe items now—the Azure DevOps extension, SSH setup, tldr and tldrf, plus gh-dash—and explicitly report on the two bash-only extensions rather than silently dropping them or overstepping.
.specstory/history/2026-07-10_06-25-00Z-at-users-david-local.md:2725:Given the constraints around run_onchange re-runs and authentication, I'll focus on reporting what's actually installed: the Windows repo has zero gh extensions currently, while the parent has three (gh-dash, gh-notify, gh-select). The key issue is that gh-dash has Windows binaries so it would work, but gh-notify and gh-select are bash-script extensions that won't run natively in PowerShell.
.specstory/history/2026-07-10_06-25-00Z-at-users-david-local.md:2876:Render+parse packages template, load profile fragment in pwsh, docs-build --strict. Report gh-extension finding (none in Windows repo; parent has gh-dash/gh-notify/gh-select) with Windows-compat caveats and offer.
.specstory/history/2026-07-10_06-25-00Z-at-users-david-local.md:3447:The compatibility issue is clear: `gh-dash` is a Go binary with Windows releases so it works fine, but `gh-notify` and `gh-select` are bash scripts that won't run natively in PowerShell. Installing extensions also requires `gh auth status` to succeed first, which won't be true on a fresh system apply, and the run_onchange mechanism won't auto-retry if it skips due to authentication. Given these constraints, I'm recommending we add only `gh-dash` with proper guards for authentication, and skip the two bash-only extensions.
.specstory/history/2026-07-10_06-25-00Z-at-users-david-local.md:3483:| `meiji163/gh-notify` | ❌ **no** — bash-script extension (needs bash + fzf); won't run natively in pwsh. |

Skip gh-notify + gh-select as bash-only. I'll mirror docs.

 `https://appriseit.com/services/windows/`

```text
- Dependency: Install the Windows COM helper. The docs state: "pip install pywin32"

- URL syntax: use the bare schema: "windows://"
  - Example URLs shown: "windows://" and "windows://?duration=5"

- Supported parameters / limits (quoted exact items, paraphrased otherwise):
  - "Image Support: Yes"
  - "Attachment Support: No"
  - Message length: "Body: 250"
  - duration: "By default this value is set to `12`" — the optional parameter name is duration (seconds).
  - No other service-specific options are documented for this schema.

- Platform limitation (quoted): "This only works if you’re sending the notification to the same windows system you’re currently accessing."
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1 — merge a managed overlay into
3	# ~/.claude/settings.json so claude-hud (statusline HUD), the pyright-lsp
4	# plugin, and a couple of safe defaults deploy on Windows. Companion to the
5	# parent (macOS/Linux) repo's dot_claude/modify_settings.json; that repo uses a
6	# `modify_` + jq merge, which is unreliable on Windows (invariant #5), so we use
7	# the same run_onchange pwsh-merger idiom as the editor overlays instead.
8	#
9	# Why a merge (not a managed dot_claude/settings.json)? Claude Code rewrites
10	# this file at runtime (plugins it installs, permissions the user grants). A
11	# static managed file would clobber all of that on every apply. We instead
12	# recursively deep-merge only our keys in, preserving every live key.
13	#
14	# Gated on installCodingAgents — no Claude Code, nothing to configure. Re-runs
15	# when the embedded overlay (claude/settings-overlay.json) changes.
16	{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}
17	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'   # best-effort; never abort the apply
18	
19	# Embedded at render time from the (chezmoi-ignored) claude/ source, so this
20	# script is self-contained and re-fires when the overlay changes.
21	$overlayJson = @'
22	{{ include "claude/settings-overlay.json" }}
23	'@
24	
25	$overlay = $overlayJson | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable
26	
27	# Recursive deep-merge: overlay leaves win; live-only keys are preserved. Unlike
28	# the editor overlay's shallow top-level assign, this keeps the user's nested
29	# state (e.g. permissions.allow/deny arrays, other enabledPlugins) intact.
30	function Merge-Hashtable([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
31	    foreach ($k in $overlay.Keys) {
32	        if ($base.ContainsKey($k) -and ($base[$k] -is [hashtable]) -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
33	            Merge-Hashtable $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
34	        } else {
35	            $base[$k] = $overlay[$k]
36	        }
37	    }
38	}
39	
40	# Respect CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (matches the statusLine command + claude-hud setup).
41	$claudeDir = if ($env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) { $env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR } else { Join-Path $HOME '.claude' }
42	$settingsPath = Join-Path $claudeDir 'settings.json'
43	
44	$settings = @{}
45	if (Test-Path $settingsPath) {
46	    $raw = Get-Content -Raw $settingsPath
47	    if ($raw.Trim()) {
48	        try { $settings = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable }
49	        catch { Write-Warning "could not parse $settingsPath; not overwriting"; return }
50	    }
51	}
52	
53	Merge-Hashtable $settings $overlay
54	New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $claudeDir | Out-Null
55	$settings | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 12 | Set-Content -Path $settingsPath -Encoding utf8
56	Write-Host "claude overlay -> $settingsPath"
57	{{ end -}}
58
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`

```text
1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1 — install the toolset with scoop (CLI) +
3	# winget (GUI). Idempotent: only missing packages are installed. This script is
4	# the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for what gets installed; re-runs whenever the
5	# rendered content (i.e. any toggle below) changes.
6	#
7	# Toggles (from .chezmoi.toml.tmpl):
8	#   installCodingAgents  = {{ .installCodingAgents }}
9	#   installWindowsApps   = {{ .installWindowsApps }}
10	#   installUtilityApps   = {{ .installUtilityApps }}
11	#   installGamingApps    = {{ .installGamingApps }}
12	#   installExtraRuntimes = {{ .installExtraRuntimes }}
13	#   installMediaTools    = {{ .installMediaTools }}
14	#   installLlmTools      = {{ .installLlmTools }}
15	#   installTunnelTools   = {{ .installTunnelTools }}
16	#   installIacTools      = {{ .installIacTools }}
17	#   installHerdr         = {{ .installHerdr }}
18	#   installClink         = {{ .installClink }}
19	#   installTry           = {{ .installTry }}
20	#   useChineseMirror     = {{ .useChineseMirror }}
21	#   managedMachine       = {{ .managedMachine }}
22	
23	# Resilient by design: individual package failures are collected and reported at
24	# the end, never fatal — one bad manifest must not abort the rest of the install
25	# (nor the rest of `chezmoi apply`). Mirrors ansible allow-partial-failure.
26	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
27	if (Test-Path variable:PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference) { $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $false }
28	$script:Failures = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
29	
30	# UTF-8 console so winget's localized output isn't mojibake on zh-* Windows.
31	try { [Console]::OutputEncoding = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false) } catch { $null = $_ }
32	{{ if .useChineseMirror }}
33	# China (GFW) mirrors for install-time package fetches.
34	$env:PIP_INDEX_URL = 'https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple'
35	$env:UV_DEFAULT_INDEX = 'https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple'
36	$env:npm_config_registry = 'https://registry.npmmirror.com'
37	$env:GOPROXY = 'https://goproxy.cn,direct'
38	$env:MISE_NODE_MIRROR_URL = 'https://npmmirror.com/mirrors/node/'
39	{{ end }}
40	
41	function Info($m) { Write-Host "==> $m" -ForegroundColor Cyan }
42	function Have($n) { [bool](Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) }
43	function Register-Failure($what) { $script:Failures.Add($what); Write-Warning "FAILED (skipped): $what" }
44	
45	# --- scoop -----------------------------------------------------------------
46	function Test-Admin {
47	    # $true when this pwsh is running elevated (member of the Administrators role).
48	    try {
49	        $id = [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()
50	        ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]$id).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
51	    } catch { $false }
52	}
53	
54	function Ensure-Scoop {
55	    if (-not (Have scoop)) {
56	        # get.scoop.sh returns install.ps1's text. Piping it to Invoke-Expression
57	        # works in a normal console, but an ELEVATED one is refused ("Running the
58	        # installer as administrator is disabled by default") unless -RunAsAdmin is
59	        # passed. Some dev boxes always open the shell as admin, so detect that and
60	        # forward the flag. We build a scriptblock from the fetched text so we can
61	        # pass args -- equivalent to `iex "& {$(irm get.scoop.sh)} -RunAsAdmin"`.
62	        $installer = [scriptblock]::Create((Invoke-RestMethod get.scoop.sh))
63	        if (Test-Admin) {
64	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (elevated console: passing -RunAsAdmin)'
65	            & $installer -RunAsAdmin
66	        } else {
67	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (user-scoped, no admin)'
68	            & $installer
69	        }
70	    }
71	    foreach ($b in 'main', 'extras', 'versions', 'nerd-fonts') {
72	        scoop bucket add $b *> $null
73	    }
74	}
75	
76	function Get-ScoopInstalled {
77	    try { return @((scoop export | ConvertFrom-Json).apps.name) } catch { return @() }
78	}
79	
80	function Scoop-Install([string[]]$apps) {
81	    if (-not $apps) { return }
82	    # Wrap in @() so a lone survivor stays an array. Without it PowerShell
83	    # unwraps a single match to a bare string, and `scoop install @missing`
84	    # then splats it one CHARACTER per arg => "Couldn't find manifest for 'm'"
85	    # when only "mise" was missing.
86	    $missing = @($apps | Where-Object { ($_ -split '/')[-1] -notin (Get-ScoopInstalled) })
87	    if (-not $missing) { Info 'scoop: all requested tools already present'; return }
88	    Info "scoop install: $($missing -join ', ')"
89	    try { scoop install @missing } catch { Write-Warning "scoop batch error: $_" }
90	    # Anything still absent after the batch = failed (scoop keeps going past a bad one).
91	    $after = Get-ScoopInstalled
92	    foreach ($app in $missing) {
93	        $name = ($app -split '/')[-1]
94	        if ($name -notin $after) { Register-Failure "scoop:$name" }
95	    }
96	}
97	
98	# --- winget ----------------------------------------------------------------
99	function Winget-Install([string]$id) {
100	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "winget:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
101	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
102	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
103	    Info "winget install $id"
104	    winget install --id $id -e --silent --scope user `
105	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
106	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
107	        # some packages don't support --scope user; retry machine scope.
108	        winget install --id $id -e --silent `
109	            --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
110	    }
111	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "winget:$id" }
112	}
113	
114	# Microsoft Store packages (installed by Store product id via the msstore
115	# source) — used for apps with no community winget manifest, e.g. Raycast and the
116	# official ChatGPT desktop app. msstore ignores --scope, so we don't pass it.
117	function Winget-InstallStore([string]$id) {
118	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
119	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
120	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
121	    Info "winget (msstore) install $id"
122	    winget install --id $id -e --source msstore --silent `
123	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
124	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id" }
125	}
126	
127	# --- npm CLI agents --------------------------------------------------------
128	function Npm-InstallGlobal([string[]]$pkgs) {
129	    if (-not (Have npm)) { foreach ($p in $pkgs) { Register-Failure "npm:$p (npm unavailable)" }; return }
130	    foreach ($p in $pkgs) {
131	        Info "npm i -g $p"
132	        npm install -g $p 2>&1 | Out-Host
133	        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "npm:$p" }
134	    }
135	}
136	
137	# --- herdr (native Windows terminal multiplexer, preview beta) --------------
138	# No scoop/winget/npm manifest — installs via herdr.dev's irm|iex bootstrap
139	# (same shape as Ensure-Scoop). Windows builds are preview-only; the bin junction
140	# is %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Herdr\bin (added to PATH in profile.d/00_env.ps1).
141	function Install-Herdr {
142	    $herdrBin = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'Programs\Herdr\bin\herdr.exe'
143	    if ((Have herdr) -or (Test-Path -LiteralPath $herdrBin)) { Info 'herdr already installed'; return }
144	    Info 'installing herdr (preview) via herdr.dev/install.ps1'
145	    try {
146	        $env:HERDR_CHANNEL = 'preview'   # Windows builds are preview-only
147	        $installer = [scriptblock]::Create((Invoke-RestMethod https://herdr.dev/install.ps1))
148	        & $installer
149	        if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $herdrBin)) { Register-Failure 'herdr' }
150	    } catch {
151	        Write-Warning "herdr install error: $_"
152	        Register-Failure 'herdr'
153	    }
154	}
155	
156	# ===========================================================================
157	Ensure-Scoop
158	
159	# Core CLI toolset — the shell baseline, always installed.
160	Scoop-Install @(
161	    'git', 'gh', 'glab', '7zip', 'make', 'just',
162	    'starship', 'zoxide', 'fzf', 'fd', 'ripgrep', 'bat', 'eza', 'jq',
163	    'delta', 'lazygit', 'neovim', 'yazi', 'btop', 'television', 'tlrc',
164	    'uv', 'gnupg',
165	    # Runtimes are NATIVE via scoop, not mise: mise's activate/shims are
166	    # unreliable on Windows and it has no windows-arm64 bun build. node + bun
167	    # here; Python via uv (below); go/rust/ruby under Extra runtimes.
168	    # copilot-proxy needs bun/bunx.
169	    'nodejs-lts', 'bun',
170	    # LazyVim pins nvim-treesitter to the `main` branch, which compiles parsers
171	    # by shelling out to `tree-sitter build`. That CLI compiles via Rust's `cc`
172	    # crate, which only knows MSVC / GCC / Clang -- NOT zig (only the retired
173	    # `master` branch accepted zig). So the parser compiler must be a real gcc on
174	    # PATH; zig here does nothing. scoop's `gcc` is nuwen MinGW-w64, amd64-only --
175	    # which also matches the amd64 nvim (native on x64, x64-emulated on ARM64), so
176	    # this ONE line covers both arches (previously gcc was ARM64-only). Plus the
177	    # tree-sitter CLI `main` requires, and win32yank so `unnamedplus` yanks reach
178	    # the Windows clipboard. (Leaner gcc alt: scoop `mingw` = niXman MinGW-w64 UCRT.)
179	    'gcc', 'tree-sitter', 'win32yank'
180	)
181	
182	# gh-dash: GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (`gh dash`). It's a gh extension, not a
183	# scoop package, so it needs gh to be authenticated -- this no-ops on a fresh
184	# machine until `gh auth login`. run_onchange only re-fires when this script
185	# changes, so after authenticating, install by hand (`gh extension install
186	# dlvhdr/gh-dash`) or force a reapply (`chezmoi state delete-bucket
187	# --bucket=entryState`). gh-notify / gh-select from the Unix repo are bash-only
188	# extensions and are intentionally skipped on Windows.
189	if (Have gh) {
190	    gh auth status *> $null
191	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
192	        $ghExts = gh extension list 2>$null | Out-String
193	        if ($ghExts -notmatch 'dlvhdr/gh-dash') {
194	            Info 'gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash'
195	            gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash
196	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'gh-ext:gh-dash' }
197	        }
198	    } else {
199	        Info 'gh not authenticated -- skipping gh-dash (gh auth login, then reapply)'
200	    }
201	}

203	# node/bun come from scoop above (on ~/scoop/shims, reliably on PATH). We do NOT
204	# use mise here -- see the runtimes note in the core scoop list.
205	
206	# Default Python, uv-managed. --default also creates unversioned python/python3
207	# (not just python3.x) in UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR (~/.local/bin, on PATH via 00_env
208	# ahead of the Store's python.exe app-alias). On uv 0.8+ `--default` lives behind
209	# preview mode, so it must be paired with `--preview` (older uv just ignored it) —
210	# without it, current uv errors: "`--default` is only available in preview mode".
211	if (Have uv) {
212	    Info 'uv python install --default --preview'
213	    $env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
214	    uv python install --default --preview 2>&1 | Out-Host
215	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:python' }
216	}
217	
218	# Make PSGallery usable non-interactively before Install-Module (NuGet provider
219	# + trust) so the fzf key bindings / audio cmdlets install reliably.
220	try {
221	    if (-not (Get-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -ListAvailable -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
222	        Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Scope CurrentUser -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
223	    }
224	    if ((Get-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).InstallationPolicy -ne 'Trusted') {
225	        Set-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -InstallationPolicy Trusted -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
226	    }
227	} catch { Write-Warning "PSGallery prep failed (non-fatal): $_" }
228	
229	# PSReadLine ships with pwsh; PSFzf powers the fzf key bindings in profile.d
230	# (Ctrl+t = files, Ctrl+r = fuzzy history search).
231	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name PSFzf)) {
232	    Info 'Install-Module PSFzf'
233	    Install-Module PSFzf -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
234	}
235	# AudioDeviceCmdlets: absolute volume + explicit mute for the sys* helpers.
236	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name AudioDeviceCmdlets)) {
237	    Info 'Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets'
238	    Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
239	}
240	
241	{{ if .installExtraRuntimes -}}
242	# Extra language runtimes, native via scoop (rust via rustup). node/bun/uv are
243	# baseline; go/rust/ruby added here.
244	Scoop-Install @('go', 'rustup', 'ruby')
245	{{ end -}}
246	
247	{{ if .installMediaTools -}}
248	Scoop-Install @('ffmpeg', 'imagemagick')
249	{{ end -}}
250	
251	{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}
252	# AI coding agents. Claude Code ships a native Windows build via winget
253	# (Anthropic.ClaudeCode — distinct from Anthropic.Claude, the desktop app in the
254	# GUI block below), so it no longer rides on npm; upgrades come with
255	# `just upgrade-winget`. The rest are npm-based CLIs; Antigravity IDE is winget.
256	Winget-Install 'Anthropic.ClaudeCode'
257	Npm-InstallGlobal @(
258	    'opencode-ai',
259	    '@openai/codex',
260	    '@github/copilot'
261	)
262	# NOTE: SpecStory ships no native-Windows CLI (macOS: brew; Linux/WSL: GitHub
263	# release binaries only), so it is intentionally omitted -- `npm i -g
264	# @specstory/cli` 404s. The Copilot module already no-ops when `specstory` is
265	# absent. Experimental Windows build from the unmerged PR #191:
266	# `just specstory-build` (see backlog/specstory-windows-native-cli.md).
267	
268	# OpenCode Desktop (GUI companion to the opencode-ai CLI) via scoop extras.
269	Scoop-Install @('extras/opencode-desktop')
270	{{ end -}}
271	
272	{{ if .installWindowsApps -}}
273	# GUI apps + editors via winget.
274	foreach ($id in @(
275	    'Microsoft.VisualStudioCode',
276	    'Anysphere.Cursor',
277	    'Microsoft.WindowsTerminal',
278	    'Alacritty.Alacritty',
279	    'wez.wezterm',
280	    'Microsoft.PowerToys',
281	    'Google.Antigravity',
282	    'Docker.DockerDesktop',
283	    'Discord.Discord',
284	    # AI desktop app + browsers.
285	    'Anthropic.Claude',
286	    'Google.Chrome',
287	    'TheBrowserCompany.Arc',
288	    'Zen-Team.Zen-Browser'
289	{{- if not .managedMachine }},
290	    'Grammarly.Grammarly'   # skipped on managed machines (often policy-blocked)
291	{{- end }}
292	)) { Winget-Install $id }
293	
294	# Store-only apps: Raycast (default launcher) + the official ChatGPT desktop app.
295	# Neither has a community winget manifest, so they install by Store product id.
296	# Codex is the npm @openai/codex CLI (coding-agents block above), not a Store app.
297	# NB: 9PLM9XGG6VKS is the OpenAI "ChatGPT" listing; the old 9NT1R1C2HH7J was
298	# "ChatGPT Classic", which winget's msstore source no longer resolves ("no
299	# matching package"). Confirm live ids with `winget search --source msstore ...`.
300	Winget-InstallStore '9PFXXSHC64H3'   # Raycast
301	Winget-InstallStore '9PLM9XGG6VKS'   # ChatGPT (OpenAI)
302	
303	# Nerd Fonts so starship glyphs render. Hack-NF-Mono supplies the
304	# "Hack Nerd Font Mono" family the Alacritty / Windows Terminal / editor
305	# configs reference; Hack-NF ("Hack Nerd Font") is the proportional companion.
306	Scoop-Install @('Hack-NF', 'Hack-NF-Mono')
307	
308	Write-Warning 'Raycast is the default launcher but shares Alt+Space with PowerToys Run -- disable PowerToys Run (PowerToys Settings > PowerToys Run > off) or rebind a hotkey. Raycast for Windows is a Store beta; if it fails to install, Flow Launcher is the fallback: winget install Flow-Launcher.Flow-Launcher.'
309	{{ end -}}
310	
311	{{ if .installGamingApps -}}
312	Winget-Install 'Valve.Steam'
313	{{ end -}}
314	
315	{{ if .installUtilityApps -}}
316	# System / utility apps via winget.
317	foreach ($id in @(
318	    'CPUID.CPU-Z',
319	    'TechPowerUp.GPU-Z',
320	    'REALiX.HWiNFO',
321	    'JAMSoftware.TreeSize.Free',
322	    'WinDirStat.WinDirStat',
323	    'voidtools.Everything',
324	    'ShareX.ShareX',
325	    'VideoLAN.VLC',
326	    'OBSProject.OBSStudio',
327	    'Rufus.Rufus'
328	{{- if not .managedMachine }},
329	    'Tailscale.Tailscale'   # skipped on managed machines (org policy blocks the MSI, exit 1625)
330	{{- end }}
331	)) { Winget-Install $id }
332	{{ end -}}
333	
334	{{ if .installLlmTools -}}
335	# Local LLM tools: Ollama (winget) + LiteLLM (uv tool).
336	Winget-Install 'Ollama.Ollama'
337	if (Have uv) { Info 'uv tool install litellm'; uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:litellm' } }
338	{{ end -}}
339	
340	{{ if .installTunnelTools -}}

342	{{ end -}}
343	
344	{{ if .installIacTools -}}

346	Winget-Install 'Microsoft.AzureCLI'
347	# Azure DevOps CLI (az extension -> `az devops`, `az repos`, `az pipelines`).
348	# It also auto-installs on first `az devops`/`az repos` use; pre-adding here just
349	# skips the first-use prompt. Skipped when az isn't on PATH yet (a fresh winget
350	# install needs a new shell) -- harmless, since first use self-installs it.
351	if (Have az) {
352	    Info 'az extension add azure-devops'
353	    az extension add --name azure-devops --upgrade --only-show-errors
354	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'az-ext:azure-devops' }
355	}
356	{{ end -}}
357	
358	{{ if .installHerdr -}}
359	Install-Herdr
360	{{ end -}}
361	
362	{{ if .installClink -}}
363	# Clink = Bash-style line editing for cmd.exe, so starship / zoxide / fzf work in
364	# the DOS prompt too (scoop main bucket). pwsh stays the default shell; this is an
365	# opt-in secondary target with prompt + navigation parity only. Our starship.lua
366	# deploys via chezmoi (AppData/Local/clink/starship.lua). The zoxide / fzf Clink
367	# bridges have no scoop/winget manifest (and zoxide has no native cmd target), so
368	# -- like herdr -- we fetch them from upstream into the Clink profile dir at apply
369	# time. Network/offline failures are collected, never fatal; starship still works.
370	Scoop-Install @('clink')
371	if (Have clink) {
372	    clink autorun install *> $null                 # HKCU AutoRun; inject into every cmd.exe (no admin)
373	    $clinkDir = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'clink'
374	    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $clinkDir | Out-Null
375	    $clinkPlugins = @{
376	        'zoxide.lua' = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shunsambongi/clink-zoxide/master/zoxide.lua'
377	        'fzf.lua'    = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chrisant996/clink-fzf/master/fzf.lua'
378	    }
379	    foreach ($name in $clinkPlugins.Keys) {
380	        Info "clink plugin: $name"
381	        try { Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Uri $clinkPlugins[$name] -OutFile (Join-Path $clinkDir $name) }
382	        catch { Register-Failure "clink-plugin:$name" }
383	    }
384	    # fzf.default_bindings is a setting DEFINED INSIDE fzf.lua, and `clink set`
385	    # reloads the profile scripts first -- so this must run AFTER fzf.lua is
386	    # downloaded above, else the setting is unknown and the value is silently
387	    # not persisted (the *> $null swallows the "unknown setting" error).
388	    clink set fzf.default_bindings true *> $null    # Ctrl-R history / Ctrl-T files / Alt-C dir
389	} else { Register-Failure 'clink (autorun/plugins not configured)' }
390	{{ end -}}
391	
392	{{ if .installTry -}}
393	# try (tobi/try) — Ruby ephemeral-workspace CLI (gem try-cli). Ruby may already
394	# be present via installExtraRuntimes; install it regardless so installTry is
395	# self-contained (Scoop-Install no-ops if ruby is already there). The pwsh
396	# integration lives in profile.d/32_try.ps1 (command: `tri`).
397	Scoop-Install @('ruby')

402	{{- else }}
403	    gem install try-cli 2>&1 | Out-Host
404	{{- end }}
405	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'gem:try-cli' }
406	} else { Register-Failure 'gem:try-cli (ruby/gem unavailable)' }
407	{{ end -}}
408	
409	if ($script:Failures.Count -gt 0) {
410	    Write-Warning ("package install: {0} item(s) failed and were skipped:" -f $script:Failures.Count)
411	    $script:Failures | ForEach-Object { Write-Warning "  - $_" }
412	    Write-Warning "Re-run 'just apply' (or 'chezmoi apply') to retry, or install them manually."
413	} else {
414	    Info 'package install complete — no failures'
415	}

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	# Claude Code notification hook using apprise
5	# Notifications are configured in ~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml
6	
7	# Read JSON from stdin
8	json="$(cat)"
9	
10	# Parse fields
11	event="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.hook_event_name // "unknown"')"
12	title="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.title // "Claude Code"')"
13	msg="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.message // empty')"
14	ntype="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.notification_type // empty')"
15	
16	# Handle Stop event (no message/title in payload)
17	if [[ "$event" == "Stop" ]]; then
18	  title="Claude Code"
19	  msg="Task finished"
20	fi
21	
22	# Build full title with notification type
23	full_title="$title"
24	[[ -n "$ntype" ]] && full_title="$title [$ntype]"
25	
26	# Send notification via apprise using desktop tag
27	# Desktop notifications are auto-detected by OS in apprise.yaml
28	apprise --tag desktop --title "$full_title" --body "$msg" 2>/dev/null || true
29
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_claude/modify_settings.json`

```text
1	#!/bin/sh
2	# chezmoi modify_ script: hook-aware deep-merge of a managed overlay into
3	# ~/.claude/settings.json.
4	#
5	# Why hook-aware? Claude Code rewrites this file at runtime AND CodeIsland
6	# (https://github.com/wxtsky/CodeIsland, macOS notch HUD) auto-installs its
7	# own entries into hooks.Notification / hooks.PermissionRequest / hooks.Stop
8	# / hooks.PreToolUse / etc. workmux's `wm setup` ALSO writes Stop /
9	# UserPromptSubmit / Notification hook entries (see docs/tools/workmux.md
10	# → "Claude leak"). A naive `jq '. * $overlay'` deep-merge replaces arrays
11	# wholesale, so declaring our notify.sh hook in the overlay would silently
12	# delete every CodeIsland / workmux entry on each `chezmoi apply` (and the
13	# tools would re-install on next launch — endless ping-pong).
14	#
15	# How: For all keys EXCEPT .hooks, do the normal recursive object merge.
16	# For .hooks.<event> arrays, additively merge — append overlay entries that
17	# aren't already present (matched by .hooks[0].command), preserving every
18	# entry the live file already contains. This lets our overlay enforce
19	# notify.sh + workmux status updates while CodeIsland / workmux setup /
20	# any other tool freely manage their own parallel entries in the same
21	# event arrays.
22	#
23	# Workmux integration: We declare the workmux Stop / SubagentStop /
24	# UserPromptSubmit / Notification hooks here directly so they're enforced
25	# on every chezmoi apply WITHOUT requiring users to also run `wm setup`.
26	# The `command -v workmux` guard lets the hook no-op silently on machines
27	# where workmux isn't installed yet (fresh box, Linux without ansible run,
28	# etc.). This patches Claude's by-design 🤖-leak: Anthropic's Stop hook
29	# fires on every turn end, but workmux's `working` state never auto-clears
30	# without an explicit `done` set.
31	#
32	# To manage an additional hook event: add it to $overlay.hooks below.
33	# To manage an additional non-hook key: add it to $overlay.
34	# Requires: jq (installed by the `base` ansible role). On a fresh-box first
35	# apply, jq isn't yet installed; this script falls back to passing the live
36	# file through unchanged. See pitfalls/modify-script-jq-bootstrap-cycle.md.
37	#
38	# See: docs/tools/agent-overlays.md → "CodeIsland integration".
39	set -eu
40	
41	overlay=$(cat <<'JSON'
42	{
43	  "hooks": {
44	    "Notification": [
45	      {
46	        "matcher": "",
47	        "hooks": [
48	          {
49	            "type": "command",
50	            "command": "~/.claude/hooks/notify.sh"
51	          }
52	        ]
53	      },
54	      {
55	        "matcher": "",
56	        "hooks": [
57	          {
58	            "type": "command",
59	            "command": "command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status waiting || true"
60	          }
61	        ]
62	      }
63	    ],
64	    "UserPromptSubmit": [
65	      {
66	        "matcher": "",
67	        "hooks": [
68	          {
69	            "type": "command",
70	            "command": "command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status working || true"
71	          }
72	        ]
73	      }
74	    ],
75	    "Stop": [
76	      {
77	        "hooks": [
78	          {
79	            "type": "command",
80	            "command": "~/.claude/hooks/notify.sh"
81	          }
82	        ]
83	      },
84	      {
85	        "hooks": [
86	          {
87	            "type": "command",
88	            "command": "command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status done || true"
89	          }
90	        ]
91	      }
92	    ],
93	    "SubagentStop": [
94	      {
95	        "hooks": [
96	          {
97	            "type": "command",
98	            "command": "command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status done || true"
99	          }
100	        ]
101	      }
102	    ]
103	  },
104	  "enabledPlugins": {
105	    "pyright-lsp@claude-plugins-official": true,
106	    "claude-hud@claude-hud": true
107	  },
108	  "extraKnownMarketplaces": {
109	    "claude-hud": {
110	      "source": {
111	        "source": "github",
112	        "repo": "jarrodwatts/claude-hud"
113	      }
114	    }
115	  },
116	  "skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt": true,
117	  "permissions": {
118	    "defaultMode": "auto"
119	  },
120	  "statusLine": {
121	    "type": "command",
122	    "command": "bash -c 'plugin_dir=$(ls -d \"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}\"/plugins/cache/claude-hud/claude-hud/*/ 2>/dev/null | sort -V | tail -1); runtime=$(command -v bun 2>/dev/null || command -v node 2>/dev/null); [ -z \"$runtime\" ] && exit 0; rt=\"${runtime##*/}\"; if [ \"$rt\" = \"bun\" ]; then exec \"$runtime\" --env-file /dev/null \"${plugin_dir}src/index.ts\"; else exec \"$runtime\" \"${plugin_dir}dist/index.js\"; fi'"
123	  }
124	}
125	JSON
126	)
127	
128	base=$(cat)
129	[ -z "$base" ] && base='{}'
130	
131	# Cold-start fallback: jq is installed by the `base` ansible role, which
132	# runs AFTER this file-apply phase. Pass the live file through unchanged
133	# and warn — the next apply (after ansible) does the real merge.
134	if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
135	  printf '%s\n' "modify_settings.json: jq not found; passing live file through unchanged. Re-run \`chezmoi apply\` after the base ansible role installs jq." >&2
136	  printf '%s' "$base"
137	  exit 0
138	fi
139	
140	# Hook-aware merge:
141	#   1. Compute non-hook portion of overlay (everything except .hooks).
142	#   2. Deep-merge that into base normally: base * overlay_no_hooks.
143	#   3. For each event in overlay.hooks, append entries whose .hooks[0].command
144	#      isn't already represented in the live event array (by exact-string
145	#      command match on .hooks[0].command). Live entries are preserved
146	#      verbatim, and new overlay entries are added at the end.
147	printf '%s' "$base" | jq --argjson overlay "$overlay" '
148	  . as $base
149	  | ($overlay | del(.hooks)) as $non_hook_overlay
150	  | ($overlay.hooks // {}) as $overlay_hooks
151	  | ($base * $non_hook_overlay) as $merged
152	  | $merged
153	  | .hooks = (
154	      ($merged.hooks // {}) as $live_hooks
155	      | reduce ($overlay_hooks | to_entries[]) as $ev (
156	          $live_hooks;
157	          .[$ev.key] = (
158	            (.[$ev.key] // []) as $live_arr
159	            | ($live_arr | map(.hooks[0].command? // "")) as $live_cmds
160	            | $live_arr + (
161	                $ev.value
162	                | map(select(
163	                    (.hooks[0].command? // "") as $cmd
164	                    | ($cmd == "") or (($live_cmds | index($cmd)) == null)
165	                  ))
166	              )
167	          )
168	        )
169	    )
170	'
171
```
</details></tool-use>

```text

1	---
2	# Python CLI tools installed via uv tool install
3	# Each tool has:
4	#   - name: package name (can include extras like [cli])
5	#   - binary: executable name to check for idempotency
6	#   - with: (optional) additional dependency to install with --with
7	#   - with_executables_from: (optional) list of packages to install AND
8	#     expose their entry points in ~/.local/bin (e.g. jupyter-core to get
9	#     the `jupyter` meta-CLI; `notebook` to get `jupyter-notebook`)
10	#     NOTE: requires uv >= 0.8.5 (--with-executables-from added in that release)
11	#     For the meta-CLI dispatch model + the 8 jupyter binaries this exposes,
12	#     see docs/tools/notebooks.md "The `jupyter` meta-CLI vs standalone binaries".
13	#     For the auto-upgrade dispatch when uv is too old, see docs/this_repo/uv-bootstrap.md.
14	#   - extra_binaries: (optional) list of additional binary names that must
15	#     also exist in ~/.local/bin for the install to be considered complete.
16	#     Use this whenever `with_executables_from` is set, so adding a new
17	#     entry-point package later triggers a `--force` reinstall on machines
18	#     that already have `binary` present from a prior apply. Without this,
19	#     ansible's `creates:` guard short-circuits the install command and the
20	#     new exec shims are never written. See
21	#     pitfalls/uv-tool-install-creates-guard-misses-executables-from.md.
22	#   - python: (optional) pin Python version (e.g. "3.11" for thefuck)
23	#   - needs_modern_gcc: (optional, default false) tool transitively pulls
24	#     numpy 2.x / pandas 3.x / pyarrow which require GCC ≥ 9.3 to compile
25	#     from source. Skipped on systems where GCC < 9 (e.g. CentOS 7's
26	#     gcc 4.8.5). uv falls back to source build because numpy 2.x
27	#     dropped manylinux_2_17 wheels — see
28	#     pitfalls/centos7-numpy-pandas-source-build.md.
29	
30	python_uv_tools:
31	  - name: thefuck
32	    binary: thefuck
33	    python: "3.11"  # Python 3.13 removed distutils which thefuck still uses
34	  - name: apprise
35	    binary: apprise
36	  - name: sqlit-tui[ssh]
37	    binary: sqlit
38	    needs_modern_gcc: true  # textual-fastdatatable -> pandas -> numpy 2.x
39	    with:
40	      - psycopg2-binary
41	      - pymysql
42	      - mssql-python
43	      - pyodbc
44	      - duckdb
45	  - name: python-dotenv[cli]
46	    binary: dotenv
47	  - name: git-filter-repo
48	    binary: git-filter-repo
49	  - name: mlflow
50	    binary: mlflow
51	    needs_modern_gcc: true  # sqlalchemy -> greenlet C++ ext needs proper C++11 (CentOS 7 gcc 4.8.5 lacks it by default)
52	  - name: tmuxp
53	    binary: tmuxp
54	  - name: visidata
55	    binary: vd
56	    needs_modern_gcc: true  # explicit pandas + pyarrow deps
57	    with:
58	      - pandas
59	      - pyarrow  # Required for parquet/feather/arrow loaders. Note: .feather
60	                 # files whose schema metadata marks columns as pandas
61	                 # StringDtype still crash the default PandasSheet path even
62	                 # with pyarrow installed; the dot_visidatarc override
63	                 # routes .feather → ArrowSheet to dodge that. See
64	                 # pitfalls/visidata-feather-stringdtype-numpy-dtype.md.
65	  - name: copyparty
66	    binary: copyparty
67	  - name: trafilatura
68	    binary: trafilatura
69	  - name: marimo[recommended,mcp]
70	    binary: marimo
71	    needs_modern_gcc: true  # marimo[recommended] -> polars[pyarrow] -> pyarrow -> numpy 2.x
72	    with:
73	      - httpx[socks]
74	  - name: jupyterlab
75	    binary: jupyter-lab
76	    needs_modern_gcc: true  # ipykernel -> pyzmq -> Cython-generated C99 (gcc 4.8.5 defaults to gnu89); also marimo[sandbox]/[recommended] chains can pull pyarrow
77	    with:
78	      - marimo[sandbox]
79	      - marimo-jupyter-extension
80	      - ipykernel
81	      - ipywidgets
82	    with_executables_from:
83	      - notebook       # exposes jupyter-notebook (classic UI, Notebook 7)
84	      - jupyter-core   # exposes the `jupyter` meta-CLI dispatcher
85	    extra_binaries:

87	      - jupyter-notebook  # from notebook
88	  # Xonsh: Python-superset shell. Installed as an experimental tertiary shell —
89	  # never set as login shell (primaryShell schema stays zsh|bash). The bundled
90	  # xontribs ship inside xonsh's tool venv so `xontrib load <name>` finds them
91	  # without extra pip work. See docs/shells/xonsh.md.

97	      - xontrib-pipeliner     # pipe shell output through Python expressions (xonsh selling point)
98	      - xontrib-fzf-widgets   # Ctrl+R / Ctrl+T fzf widgets (uses fzf binary on PATH)

```text
1	# Backlog: scheduled audit monitoring with apprise
2	
3	**Status**: design / not implemented. Captured 2026-05 from a user
4	question after `audit-summary` / `health-check` shipped.
5	
6	## Problem
7	
8	Helpers like `health-check`, `audit-summary`, `audit-failed-logins`,
9	`fw-listening` are designed for *ad-hoc* sysadmin queries. To turn
10	them into a real monitoring story you need three things this repo
11	doesn't yet have:
12	
13	1. **Scheduling** — run the check at a regular cadence without a human.
14	2. **Diff awareness** — only alert when the answer **changes**
15	   (otherwise the morning email becomes noise the user filters out
16	   on day 3).
17	3. **Notification routing** — push the result to wherever the user
18	   actually reads alerts (Slack, Discord, Telegram, email, ntfy,
19	   matrix, gotify, ...). Hard-coding any one channel is a footgun.
20	
21	The natural answer to (3) is **[apprise](https://github.com/caronc/apprise)**:
22	a single Python CLI that abstracts ~80 notification services behind
23	URLs (e.g. `slack://...`, `discord://...`, `tgram://...`,
24	`mailto://...`, `ntfy://...`). Already pip-installable; would slot
25	into this repo's `installPythonUvTools` ansible role with a one-line
26	addition to `dot_ansible/roles/python_uv_tools/tasks/main.yml`.
27	
28	## Proposed shape (NOT implemented)
29	
30	### A. New helper `audit-monitor`
31	
32	A wrapper that:
33	
34	1. Runs a check (delegated by name: `audit-monitor health` runs
35	   `health-check --no-color`; `audit-monitor failed-logins` runs
36	   `audit-failed-logins`; etc.)
37	2. Hashes / diffs the output against the previous run cached at
38	   `~/.cache/audit-monitor/<check>.last`.
39	3. If the output changed (or `--always`), sends via apprise to a
40	   per-check URL.
41	
42	```bash
43	audit-monitor health --notify "${APPRISE_URL_OPS}"
44	audit-monitor failed-logins --threshold 50 --notify "${APPRISE_URL_SECURITY}"
45	audit-monitor disk --threshold 90 --notify "${APPRISE_URL_OPS}"
46	```
47	
48	The `--threshold` interface is for numeric checks; non-numeric checks
49	fall back to "alert if the output diff is non-empty".
50	
51	### B. Scheduling: pueue OR systemd timer
52	
53	Two reasonable backends, user picks per host:
54	
55	- **pueue** (already in this repo via `cargo`): great for ad-hoc /
56	  user-scope; daemon is per-user; survives reboot only if the user
57	  starts pueued. Good for personal workstation morning checks.
58	  Recipe in [`docs/tools/pueue.md`](../docs/tools/pueue.md) already
59	  exists.
60	- **systemd timer**: better for true server use. One `*.timer` +
61	  `*.service` per check, OnCalendar-anchored. Survives reboot,
62	  works without an active user session, integrates with the
63	  existing `tv scheduled-jobs` channel.
64	
65	This repo ships an `auditd` ansible role; a sibling `audit_monitor`
66	role could drop:
67	
68	```text
69	/etc/systemd/system/audit-monitor-health.timer
70	/etc/systemd/system/audit-monitor-health.service
71	/etc/systemd/system/audit-monitor-disk.timer
72	/etc/systemd/system/audit-monitor-disk.service
73	...
74	```
75	
76	with a fresh `installAuditMonitor` chezmoi prompt (default false,
77	gated to Linux).
78	
79	### C. Notification URL secret management
80	
81	Hard problem; the *reason* this is in backlog rather than shipped.
82	apprise URLs contain auth secrets (`tgram://<bot-token>/<chat>`,
83	`slack://<token>/<channel>`, etc.). They MUST NOT land in:
84	
85	- `~/.config/audit-monitor/config` (would sync to every host via dotfiles)
86	- env vars in `dot_zshrc` (shell history exposure)
87	- ansible `defaults/main.yml` (commit hazard)
88	
89	Acceptable patterns in this repo's conventions (see
90	[`docs/this_repo/sudo-session.md`](../docs/this_repo/sudo-session.md)
91	and [`docs/tools/agent-overlays.md`](../docs/tools/agent-overlays.md)
92	for prior art):
93	
94	1. **Bitwarden CLI** (already integrated; `installBitwarden=true`):
95	   fetch URL on each check via `bw get notes "apprise/health"`.
96	   Pro: secrets never on disk plaintext. Con: needs unlocked
97	   session, doesn't work for boot-time / unattended timers.
98	2. **`~/.config/audit-monitor/secrets.env` mode 0600** in
99	   `.chezmoiignore.tmpl` (per-host, not synced). User manually
100	   provisions on each host. Pro: simple, works unattended. Con:
101	   manual provisioning step.
102	3. **systemd `LoadCredentialEncrypted=`** — encrypts secrets to
103	   the host's TPM/sealed key. Pro: best for true server. Con:
104	   complex, kernel/TPM-version-dependent.
105	
106	Default would be (2); document (1) for desktops and (3) for hardened
107	servers.
108	
109	### D. Per-check trigger conditions (the actual security value)
110	
111	Each check has a different "what counts as actionable" definition:
112	
113	| Check | Trigger | Rationale |
114	|---|---|---|
115	| `health-check` | overall verdict goes from OK → WARN | Catches state transitions, not steady noise |
116	| `audit-failed-logins` | `count > 50/day` OR `count > 10 from one IP` | Brute-force pattern |
117	| `audit-sudo` (with `audit-watch` patterns) | any HIGH-severity match (sudoers edit, useradd, root login) | High-confidence security event |
118	| `disk-usage` | any mount >= 90% | Disk-full pages on-call |
119	| `fw-listening` | new listener that wasn't there yesterday | Persistence implant detection |
120	| `cron-list` | new entry that wasn't there yesterday | Same |
121	| `pkg-recent` | any install in the last 24h on a "no-change" host | Supply-chain trip wire |
122	
123	The `--threshold` flag covers numeric ones; the diff-aware mode
124	covers structural ones. Both need state files in `~/.cache/audit-monitor/`.
125	
126	## Out of scope (for this backlog item)
127	
128	- **Real-time streaming alerts** (e.g. push the moment audit-watch
129	  catches a HIGH match). That's a separate "agent" daemon with very
130	  different lifecycle requirements; document as a follow-on backlog
131	  if anyone asks.
132	- **Aggregation across hosts** (fleet-wide monitoring dashboard).
133	  That's a SIEM, not a dotfile. Hand-off point: ship the apprise
134	  URL per host, route them to a shared channel, let the human / a
135	  cheap downstream rule (Slack workflow, Discord webhook into a
136	  dedicated channel) do correlation.
137	- **Acking / silencing alerts**. Apprise is fire-and-forget; it has
138	  no concept of acking. If you need that, you've outgrown this
139	  approach — go to PagerDuty / Opsgenie / similar.
140	
141	## Decision criteria before promoting from backlog
142	
143	Promote to TODO when at least two of these are true:
144	
145	1. User runs at least one of the helpers manually 3+ days in a row
146	   (signal: this is real recurring work, automation pays off).
147	2. User has a specific incident they wish they'd caught earlier.
148	3. User has a target apprise channel already (Slack workspace,
149	   Telegram bot, etc.) and is comfortable provisioning the URL.
150	
151	Until then the manual `health-check` / `audit-watch` workflow
152	covers ~90% of the value at 0% of the secret-management cost.
153	
154	## Related
155
