# How the unix/macOS dotfiles wire up Claude Code desktop notifications

The short version: **the notify script does NOT do any platform detection or backend selection itself — it unconditionally calls `apprise --tag desktop`, and all platform logic lives in the chezmoi-templated `apprise.yaml`.** And crucially for you: **the `apprise.yaml.tmpl` already has a `windows://` branch.** This repo's design is exactly the pattern you want to mirror.

## 1. The notify hook script

**File:** `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_claude/hooks/executable_notify.sh.tmpl`
- Chezmoi renders this to `~/.claude/hooks/notify.sh` (the `executable_` prefix sets +x; the `.tmpl` suffix means it's templated — though this particular file contains no template directives, so it's byte-identical on every OS).

Full file (29 lines):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Claude Code notification hook using apprise
# Notifications are configured in ~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml

# Read JSON from stdin
json="$(cat)"

# Parse fields
event="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.hook_event_name // "unknown"')"
title="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.title // "Claude Code"')"
msg="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.message // empty')"
ntype="$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.notification_type // empty')"

# Handle Stop event (no message/title in payload)
if [[ "$event" == "Stop" ]]; then
  title="Claude Code"
  msg="Task finished"
fi

# Build full title with notification type
full_title="$title"
[[ -n "$ntype" ]] && full_title="$title [$ntype]"

# Send notification via apprise using desktop tag
# Desktop notifications are auto-detected by OS in apprise.yaml
apprise --tag desktop --title "$full_title" --body "$msg" 2>/dev/null || true
```

Answers to your specific questions:

- **Backend(s):** Exactly one — **`apprise`**. No terminal-notifier, no osascript, no notify-send, no ntfy, no direct anything. Every OS goes through the single `apprise` CLI call. It relies on `jq` for parsing (installed by the `base` ansible role).
- **Does it use apprise?** Yes, and it is the *only* backend. The invocation is:
  ```
  apprise --tag desktop --title "$full_title" --body "$msg"
  ```
  It selects the target by the **`--tag desktop`** filter, not by a URL on the command line. The actual URL/service is defined in the config file `~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml` (see section 2). The `2>/dev/null || true` makes the hook non-fatal if apprise is missing or fails.
- **How it decides platform (macOS vs Linux vs Windows):** It does **not**. There is zero OS-detection logic in the script. Platform selection is fully delegated to apprise's config file, which is chezmoi-templated per-OS. This is the clean separation you'll want to copy: the hook is OS-agnostic; the config picks the backend.
- **What it reads from Claude Code's hook stdin payload:** It `cat`s the JSON from stdin and pulls four fields with `jq`:
  - `.hook_event_name` → `event` (default `"unknown"`)
  - `.title` → `title` (default `"Claude Code"`)
  - `.message` → `msg` (default empty)
  - `.notification_type` → `ntype` (default empty)
  
  Special case: when `event == "Stop"` the payload carries no title/message, so it hard-codes `title="Claude Code"` and `msg="Task finished"`. The final notification title becomes `"$title [$ntype]"` when a notification_type is present, else just `"$title"`. (Note: it does **not** read session id, cwd, transcript_path, or any other fields Claude Code sends.)

## 2. Apprise: install + config (all hits)

### How apprise is INSTALLED

**File:** `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_ansible/roles/python_uv_tools/defaults/main.yml` (lines 34-35):

```yaml
  - name: apprise
    binary: apprise
```

It is installed as a **`uv tool install apprise`** (a global uv tool, exposing the `apprise` binary in `~/.local/bin`). Not pipx, not brew, not a plain pip. The `python_uv_tools` ansible role iterates this list and runs `uv tool install` for each entry, using the `binary:` name for its idempotency `creates:` guard. Confirmed by the tool-manager docs:
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/this_repo/tool-managers.md:974`: `| **apprise** | uv tool | uv tool | python_uv_tools |`
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/this_repo/architecture.md:119`: lists apprise under the `python_uv_tools` role.
- `README.md:320`: "Python tools (via uv): thefuck, apprise, sqlit-tui, …"
- Upgrades ride along with `just upgrade-uv` → `uv tool upgrade --all` (README:364).

For Windows you'd instead do `uv tool install apprise` (or `pipx install apprise`) — same CLI, and you'd want the `windows://` extra which pulls in `win10toast` on Windows.

### How apprise is CONFIGURED

The config lives in `~/.config/apprise/` (rendered from `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/`). Three source files:

**a) `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/apprise.yaml.tmpl`** — the main config, templated per-OS. This is the heart of the platform selection and **it already contains your `windows://` case**:

```yaml
version: 1

# Include user's custom notification services
# custom.yaml is created once by chezmoi and never overwritten
# Edit it to add your own services (Discord, Slack, Telegram, etc.)
include:
  - custom.yaml

# Default system-wide desktop notifications (auto-detected by OS)
urls:
{{- if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" }}
  # macOS native notifications
  - macosx://:
      tag: desktop
{{- else if eq .chezmoi.os "linux" }}
  # Linux DBus notifications (works with most DEs)
  - dbus://:
      tag: desktop
{{- else if eq .chezmoi.os "windows" }}
  # Windows native notifications
  - windows://:
      tag: desktop
{{- end }}
```

Key mechanics to mirror:
- The `tag: desktop` on the URL is what the hook's `--tag desktop` matches against. That's the entire wiring between hook and backend.
- Per-OS `{{ if eq .chezmoi.os ... }}` selects `macosx://` / `dbus://` / `windows://`. The `windows://` URL is the standard apprise Windows toast backend (uses `win10toast` under the hood).
- It `include:`s a sibling `custom.yaml` for user-added remote services.

**b) `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/create_custom.yaml`** — the `create_` prefix means chezmoi writes it **once and never overwrites** (this is the mechanism that keeps user secrets safe, used *instead of* a `.chezmoiignore` entry — I checked `.chezmoiignore*` and custom.yaml is not listed there). Renders to `~/.config/apprise/custom.yaml`:

```yaml
# Your custom Apprise notification services
# This file is created once and never overwritten by chezmoi
# See example.yaml for configuration examples
version: 1
urls:
  # - discord://webhook_id/webhook_token:
  #     tag: chat
  []
```

**c) `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/example.yaml`** — a tracked, committed reference file (safe, no secrets) showing Discord/Slack/mailto/Telegram/Pushover/json URL examples with `tag:` groupings. Points at `https://appriseit.com/getting-started/configuration/`.

### Other apprise references (non-code)

- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/backlog/audit-monitor-with-apprise.md` — a design/research doc (NOT implemented) for a future `audit-monitor` helper that would reuse apprise for scheduled sysadmin alerts. Most relevant part for you is its **secret-management discussion (section C)**: it explicitly warns apprise URLs contain auth tokens and must not be synced via dotfiles; documents three patterns (Bitwarden CLI fetch / per-host `0600` env file in `.chezmoiignore` / systemd `LoadCredentialEncrypted=`). This is why `custom.yaml` uses the create-once pattern.
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/TODO.md:89` — the backlog item's index entry.
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/tools/agent-overlays.md:293` — documents a *future* idea to extend `notify.sh` to also fire `apprise --tag webhook` for remote chat delivery, gated behind an env var `CLAUDE_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK=1`. Not implemented, but shows the intended extension model (add more `tag:`-grouped URLs to `custom.yaml`, add a second `apprise --tag webhook` call to the hook).
- `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/backlog/tmux-window-status-indicators.md:78` — mentions reusing "the existing apprise call" for tmux status.
- Docs tables at `docs/this_repo/tool-managers.md` and `architecture.md` (+ their `.zh-TW` translations) — just catalog apprise as a uv tool.
- (The `.venv/` and `.claude/worktrees/` hits are noise — a vendored watchdog lib and duplicate worktree copies of the same files.)

## 3. The `modify_settings.json` overlay — hook events wired

**File:** `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_claude/modify_settings.json` (a chezmoi `modify_` script that hook-aware deep-merges an overlay into `~/.claude/settings.json`). Confirmed the events and commands it enforces:

| Event | Command(s) it appends |
|---|---|
| **`Notification`** | (1) `~/.claude/hooks/notify.sh` — the apprise notify. (2) `command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status waiting \|\| true` |
| **`UserPromptSubmit`** | `command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status working \|\| true` |
| **`Stop`** | (1) `~/.claude/hooks/notify.sh` — the apprise notify. (2) `command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status done \|\| true` |
| **`SubagentStop`** | `command -v workmux >/dev/null 2>&1 && workmux set-window-status done \|\| true` |

So `notify.sh` is fired on exactly two events: **`Notification`** and **`Stop`**. (SubagentStop and UserPromptSubmit only drive workmux window-status, not notifications.)

Important detail about *how* it merges (relevant if you replicate the overlay approach on Windows): it does an **additive, hook-aware merge** — for `.hooks.<event>` arrays it appends only overlay entries whose `.hooks[0].command` isn't already present, preserving entries that CodeIsland / workmux `wm setup` inject at runtime. A naive `jq '. * $overlay'` would wipe those out on every `chezmoi apply`. It also has a jq-not-yet-installed cold-start fallback (passes the live file through unchanged). The overlay additionally sets `enabledPlugins`, `extraKnownMarketplaces` (claude-hud), `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt`, `permissions.defaultMode: auto`, and a `statusLine` command.

## 4. Env vars / config paths the notify flow depends on

There are **no notification env vars** in the current implementation — I grepped for `NTFY_TOPIC`, `APPRISE`, `NTFY`, etc. in the hooks dir and `dot_zshrc*` and found nothing wired up. The design deliberately keeps zero env-var coupling. Specifically:

- **`~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml`** — the only path the hook implicitly depends on (apprise's default config search path; the comment in notify.sh names it explicitly). Contains the per-OS `--tag desktop` URL.
- **`~/.config/apprise/custom.yaml`** — included by apprise.yaml; where a user adds remote services. Create-once, never synced-over.
- **`jq`** on PATH — required by the hook to parse stdin (installed by the `base` ansible role).
- **`apprise`** on PATH (in `~/.local/bin` via uv tool) — the actual sender.
- Env vars that appear only in *unimplemented* proposals: `CLAUDE_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK=1` (docs/tools/agent-overlays.md:293), and `APPRISE_URL_OPS` / `APPRISE_URL_SECURITY` (backlog/audit-monitor). Neither is active today.

## What this means for your Windows mirror

The pattern is remarkably clean to replicate — the repo already did 90% of the Windows work:

1. Install apprise on Windows (`uv tool install apprise` or `pipx install apprise`). Ensure the Windows toast dependency is present (apprise's `windows://` uses `win10toast`).
2. Ship an OS-agnostic `notify.sh`/`notify.ps1` equivalent that just calls `apprise --tag desktop --title ... --body ...` reading the Claude hook JSON from stdin (fields `.hook_event_name`, `.title`, `.message`, `.notification_type`; Stop event → "Task finished").
3. Provide `~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml` with:
   ```yaml
   - windows://:
       tag: desktop
   ```
   — which is literally the `{{ else if eq .chezmoi.os "windows" }}` branch already present in `apprise.yaml.tmpl`.
4. Wire the Claude `Notification` and `Stop` hook events to run the script (mirror of `modify_settings.json`), ideally with the same additive-merge care if other tools also write to `~/.claude/settings.json`.
5. Keep the remote-service URLs (which carry secrets) in a create-once, non-synced `custom.yaml`, per the backlog doc's secret-management guidance.

The single most useful source file to copy is `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/apprise/apprise.yaml.tmpl` — it's already cross-platform and includes the `windows://` case.

# Findings: Claude settings overlay, package install, and notifications

## 1. The Claude settings-overlay merger

**File path:** `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl`

**Gating / templating:** The entire body is wrapped in `{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}` … `{{ end -}}`. The overlay JSON is embedded at render time via `{{ include "claude/settings-overlay.json" }}` into a PowerShell here-string, so the script is self-contained and chezmoi re-fires it whenever the overlay content changes (the `run_onchange` mechanism hashes the rendered script). It respects `$env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` (falling back to `~/.claude`), targets `settings.json`, and uses `$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'` (best-effort, never aborts the apply).

**Merge logic — this is the critical part for your plan. It is a recursive OBJECT deep-merge, but arrays are REPLACED wholesale, NOT appended.** It is NOT hook-aware. Here is the exact merge function:

```powershell
$overlay = $overlayJson | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable

# Recursive deep-merge: overlay leaves win; live-only keys are preserved.
function Merge-Hashtable([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
    foreach ($k in $overlay.Keys) {
        if ($base.ContainsKey($k) -and ($base[$k] -is [hashtable]) -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
            Merge-Hashtable $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
        } else {
            $base[$k] = $overlay[$k]
        }
    }
}
```

Behavior: recursion happens **only when both sides are hashtables (JSON objects)**. For any other type — including arrays — it takes the `else` branch: `$base[$k] = $overlay[$k]`, a wholesale replacement.

**What this means for notification hooks (important gotcha):** In Claude Code's `settings.json`, `hooks` is an object keyed by event name (`"Notification"`, `"Stop"`, `"PreToolUse"`, …), and each event's value is an **array** of matcher/hook entries. With this merger:
- `hooks` itself is a hashtable → the merge recurses into it (good — other event keys the user has live are preserved).
- But `hooks.Notification` is an **array** → the `else` branch fires and the overlay's `hooks.Notification` array **replaces** any live `hooks.Notification` array entirely. It does NOT append entries not already present.

So if you add a `hooks.Notification` entry via the overlay and the user later adds their own `Notification` hook at runtime, the next `chezmoi apply` would clobber the user's. Conversely, since the overlay is the only managed source, clobbering the overlay's own array is fine. If you need additive/append-if-absent array semantics (like the parent repo's jq merge), you must extend `Merge-Hashtable` to special-case arrays (e.g., union by serialized-entry identity). This is explicitly called out as a known limitation — the parent repo (macOS/Linux `dot_claude/modify_settings.json`) uses a jq additive-array hook merge that was deliberately NOT ported here because the current overlay has no `hooks` key (confirmed in `.claude/plans/snazzy-painting-wilkes.md` and the specstory transcript at `.specstory/history/2026-07-12_07-48-49Z-at-users-david-local.md`: "did not port the jq additive-array hook merge — the chosen overlay has no `hooks` key… If you add hooks to the overlay later, that needs revisiting.").

Rest of the script (path resolution + write):
```powershell
$claudeDir = if ($env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) { $env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR } else { Join-Path $HOME '.claude' }
$settingsPath = Join-Path $claudeDir 'settings.json'
$settings = @{}
if (Test-Path $settingsPath) {
    $raw = Get-Content -Raw $settingsPath
    if ($raw.Trim()) {
        try { $settings = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable }
        catch { Write-Warning "could not parse $settingsPath; not overwriting"; return }
    }
}
Merge-Hashtable $settings $overlay
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $claudeDir | Out-Null
$settings | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 12 | Set-Content -Path $settingsPath -Encoding utf8
```
Note `-Depth 12` on `ConvertTo-Json` — deep hook structures are within that depth, but worth remembering.

## 3. Package installation

**File path:** `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` (this is described in-file as "the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for what gets installed").

**Fault-tolerance pattern:** `$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'`, a `$script:Failures` list, and `Register-Failure($what)` which records + warns but never throws. At the end it prints a summary of all skipped failures. Helper `Have($n)` = `[bool](Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)`.

**Representative install patterns (each wired to Register-Failure):**

- **scoop** — batch install of only-missing apps; re-checks after and registers any still-absent:
```powershell
function Scoop-Install([string[]]$apps) {
    $missing = @($apps | Where-Object { ($_ -split '/')[-1] -notin (Get-ScoopInstalled) })
    if (-not $missing) { Info 'scoop: all requested tools already present'; return }
    try { scoop install @missing } catch { Write-Warning "scoop batch error: $_" }
    $after = Get-ScoopInstalled
    foreach ($app in $missing) {
        $name = ($app -split '/')[-1]
        if ($name -notin $after) { Register-Failure "scoop:$name" }
    }
}
```
Buckets added by `Ensure-Scoop`: `main`, `extras`, `versions`, `nerd-fonts`.

- **winget** — idempotent (skip if `winget list` succeeds), user scope then machine-scope retry, else register:
```powershell
function Winget-Install([string]$id) {
    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "winget:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }
    winget install --id $id -e --silent --scope user --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { winget install --id $id -e --silent --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host }
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "winget:$id" }
}
```
There is also `Winget-InstallStore` for `--source msstore` product-id installs (Raycast, ChatGPT).

- **npm global** (used only for coding agents):
```powershell
function Npm-InstallGlobal([string[]]$pkgs) {
    if (-not (Have npm)) { foreach ($p in $pkgs) { Register-Failure "npm:$p (npm unavailable)" }; return }
    foreach ($p in $pkgs) {
        npm install -g $p 2>&1 | Out-Host
        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "npm:$p" }
    }
}
```

- **PSGallery / Install-Module** — non-interactive prep (NuGet provider + trust PSGallery) then per-module guarded `Install-Module … -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense`:
```powershell
if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name PSFzf)) {
    Install-Module PSFzf -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
}
```
(Same shape for `AudioDeviceCmdlets`.)

**Python / pip / pipx / uv — YES, `uv` is the Python mechanism (this is your apprise install path):**
- `uv` is installed via **scoop** in the always-installed core toolset (line 164: `'uv', 'gnupg',`).
- Python itself is installed **by uv**, not scoop/winget (lines 211–216):
```powershell
if (Have uv) {
    $env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
    uv python install --default --preview 2>&1 | Out-Host
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:python' }
}
```
- There is **no pip / pipx** anywhere. The established pattern for installing a Python CLI tool is **`uv tool install`**, already used for LiteLLM under `installLlmTools` (lines 335–337) — this is the exact template you'd copy for apprise:
```powershell
if (Have uv) { Info 'uv tool install litellm'; uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:litellm' } }
```
So `apprise` would install as `uv tool install apprise` (guarded by `if (Have uv)`), which drops the `apprise` executable into `~/.local/bin` (`UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR` / uv tool bin dir, already on PATH per `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1`). Note: apprise's Windows backend (`windows://`) needs the `pywin32` package; `uv tool install apprise` alone may not pull it — you may need `uv tool install --with pywin32 apprise` (worth verifying against apprise's Windows service docs at appriseit.com/services/windows).

**Toggle → install gating map** (from the template `{{ if .X }}` blocks):
| Toggle | Installs |
|---|---|
| (always) | core CLI incl. `uv`, `nodejs-lts`, `bun`, `gcc`, `tree-sitter`; uv Python; PSFzf; AudioDeviceCmdlets; gh-dash (if gh authed) |
| `installCodingAgents` | Claude Code (winget `Anthropic.ClaudeCode`), npm `opencode-ai`/`@openai/codex`/`@github/copilot`, scoop `opencode-desktop` — **also gates the settings-overlay merger (§1)** |
| `installExtraRuntimes` | scoop `go`, `rustup`, `ruby` |
| `installMediaTools` | scoop `ffmpeg`, `imagemagick` |
| `installWindowsApps` | GUI apps + editors + Nerd Fonts via winget/msstore |
| `installGamingApps` | winget `Valve.Steam` |
| `installUtilityApps` | CPU-Z, GPU-Z, HWiNFO, TreeSize, Everything, ShareX, VLC, OBS, Rufus, (Tailscale unless managed) |
| `installLlmTools` | winget `Ollama.Ollama` + **`uv tool install litellm[proxy]`** |
| `installTunnelTools` | scoop `ngrok`, `cloudflared` |
| `installIacTools` | scoop `terraform`, `opentofu`; winget Azure CLI; `az extension add azure-devops` |
| `installHerdr` | `Install-Herdr` (irm\|iex bootstrap) |
| `installClink` | scoop `clink` + Lua plugin fetches |
| `installTry` | scoop `ruby` + `gem install try-cli` |
| `useChineseMirror` | sets `PIP_INDEX_URL`, `UV_DEFAULT_INDEX`, npm/go/node mirror env vars at top of script |
| `managedMachine` | *excludes* Grammarly / Tailscale |

## 4. Init prompts in `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl`

**File path:** `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.chezmoi.toml.tmpl`. The `[data]` block uses helper funcs `promptBoolOnce`, `promptStringOnce`, `promptChoiceOnce` (the `Once` variants persist answers to `%USERPROFILE%\.config\chezmoi\chezmoi.toml` and never re-ask). `$full := eq $role "workstation"` is the common default for "full desktop" toggles.

Full list of `installX` / toggle prompts (name + exact prompt text + default):

- `role` (choice `workstation|minimal`, default `workstation`) — "Role: workstation (full desktop) or minimal (shell only)"
- `name` (string, "Da-Wei Lee") — "Your full name (git)"
- `email` (string) — "Your git email"
- `installCodingAgents` (bool, default `$full`) — "Install coding agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Copilot CLI, SpecStory)"
- `installWindowsApps` (bool, `$full`) — "Install GUI apps via winget (VSCode, Cursor, Notepad++, Terminal, Alacritty, PowerToys, Raycast)"
- `installWsl` (bool, `$full`) — "Install WSL2 (Docker Desktop backend; needs admin + reboot)"
- `installWslUbuntu` (bool, `false`) — "Install a WSL2 Ubuntu distro and bootstrap cross-platform dotfiles inside it (needs admin; requires WSL2 platform)"
- `wslUsername` (string, `.chezmoi.username | lower`) — "WSL Ubuntu username"
- `wslUbuntuBootstrap` (choice `headless|interactive|none`, `headless`) — "WSL Ubuntu dotfiles bootstrap (headless|interactive|none)"
- `installUtilityApps` (bool, `$full`) — "Install utility apps via winget (CPU-Z, GPU-Z, TreeSize, VLC, Everything, ShareX, HWiNFO)"
- `installGamingApps` (bool, `false`) — "Install gaming apps (Steam)"
- `installExtraRuntimes` (bool, `$full`) — "Install extra runtimes (rust, go, ruby) beyond node/bun/uv"
- `installMediaTools` (bool, `false`) — "Install media CLIs (ffmpeg, imagemagick)"
- `installLlmTools` (bool, `false`) — "Install local LLM tools (Ollama, LiteLLM)"
- `installTunnelTools` (bool, `false`) — "Install tunnel tools (ngrok, cloudflared)"
- `installIacTools` (bool, `false`) — "Install Infrastructure-as-Code tools (Azure CLI, Terraform, OpenTofu)"
- `installSshServer` (bool, `false`) — "Install and enable the OpenSSH server / sshd (needs admin, opens inbound port 22)"
- `installHerdr` (bool, `false`) — "Install herdr (native Windows terminal multiplexer, preview beta)"
- `installClink` (bool, `false`) — "Install Clink + starship/zoxide/fzf for cmd.exe (DOS prompt)"
- `installTry` (bool, `false`) — "Install try (Ruby ephemeral-workspace CLI, needs Ruby)"
- `useChineseMirror` (bool, `false`) — "Use China (GFW) mirrors for pip/npm/cargo/go/node"
- `managedMachine` (bool, `false`) — "Managed or corporate machine (skip org-policy-blocked apps like Tailscale and Grammarly)"
- `backupMode` (choice `smart|full|off`, `smart`) — "Back up existing files before apply (smart|full|off)"
- `enableVimMode` (bool, `true`) — "Enable PSReadLine vi editing mode"

**Pattern for adding `installApprise`:** copy one `promptBoolOnce` line, e.g.
`installApprise = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installApprise" "Install apprise for desktop notifications (uv tool)" false }}`
Then reference `.installApprise` in the packages template and/or gate the overlay-hooks addition. **However**, given apprise notifications are only meaningful with Claude Code (whose hooks fire them) and the overlay merger is already gated on `installCodingAgents`, the simplest design is to **piggyback on `installCodingAgents`** rather than add a new toggle — install `apprise` via `uv tool install` inside the existing `{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}` block in the packages script, and add the `hooks` key to `settings-overlay.json` (also `installCodingAgents`-gated). A separate `installApprise` toggle only earns its keep if you want notifications independent of coding agents. Note also that the toggle must be added to the `.installLlmTools`-style docs list AND (for clarity) to the comment header block at the top of the packages template (lines 7–22) which mirrors every toggle.

 `./.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl`

```text
1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1 — merge a managed overlay into
3	# ~/.claude/settings.json so claude-hud (statusline HUD), the pyright-lsp
4	# plugin, and a couple of safe defaults deploy on Windows. Companion to the
5	# parent (macOS/Linux) repo's dot_claude/modify_settings.json; that repo uses a
6	# `modify_` + jq merge, which is unreliable on Windows (invariant #5), so we use
7	# the same run_onchange pwsh-merger idiom as the editor overlays instead.
8	#
9	# Why a merge (not a managed dot_claude/settings.json)? Claude Code rewrites
10	# this file at runtime (plugins it installs, permissions the user grants). A
11	# static managed file would clobber all of that on every apply. We instead
12	# recursively deep-merge only our keys in, preserving every live key.
13	#
14	# Gated on installCodingAgents — no Claude Code, nothing to configure. Re-runs
15	# when the embedded overlay (claude/settings-overlay.json) changes.
16	{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}
17	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'   # best-effort; never abort the apply
18	
19	# Embedded at render time from the (chezmoi-ignored) claude/ source, so this
20	# script is self-contained and re-fires when the overlay changes.
21	$overlayJson = @'
22	{{ include "claude/settings-overlay.json" }}
23	'@
24	
25	$overlay = $overlayJson | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable
26	
27	# Recursive deep-merge: overlay leaves win; live-only keys are preserved. Unlike
28	# the editor overlay's shallow top-level assign, this keeps the user's nested
29	# state (e.g. permissions.allow/deny arrays, other enabledPlugins) intact.
30	function Merge-Hashtable([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
31	    foreach ($k in $overlay.Keys) {
32	        if ($base.ContainsKey($k) -and ($base[$k] -is [hashtable]) -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
33	            Merge-Hashtable $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
34	        } else {
35	            $base[$k] = $overlay[$k]
36	        }
37	    }
38	}
39	
40	# Respect CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (matches the statusLine command + claude-hud setup).
41	$claudeDir = if ($env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) { $env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR } else { Join-Path $HOME '.claude' }
42	$settingsPath = Join-Path $claudeDir 'settings.json'
43	
44	$settings = @{}
45	if (Test-Path $settingsPath) {
46	    $raw = Get-Content -Raw $settingsPath
47	    if ($raw.Trim()) {
48	        try { $settings = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable }
49	        catch { Write-Warning "could not parse $settingsPath; not overwriting"; return }
50	    }
51	}
52	
53	Merge-Hashtable $settings $overlay
54	New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $claudeDir | Out-Null
55	$settings | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 12 | Set-Content -Path $settingsPath -Encoding utf8
56	Write-Host "claude overlay -> $settingsPath"
57	{{ end -}}
58
```
</details></tool-use>

28	$script:Failures = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()

39	{{ end }}
40	
41	function Info($m) { Write-Host "==> $m" -ForegroundColor Cyan }
42	function Have($n) { [bool](Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) }
43	function Register-Failure($what) { $script:Failures.Add($what); Write-Warning "FAILED (skipped): $what" }

45	# --- scoop -----------------------------------------------------------------
46	function Test-Admin {
47	    # $true when this pwsh is running elevated (member of the Administrators role).
48	    try {
49	        $id = [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()
50	        ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]$id).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
51	    } catch { $false }
52	}
53	
54	function Ensure-Scoop {
55	    if (-not (Have scoop)) {
56	        # get.scoop.sh returns install.ps1's text. Piping it to Invoke-Expression
57	        # works in a normal console, but an ELEVATED one is refused ("Running the
58	        # installer as administrator is disabled by default") unless -RunAsAdmin is
59	        # passed. Some dev boxes always open the shell as admin, so detect that and
60	        # forward the flag. We build a scriptblock from the fetched text so we can
61	        # pass args -- equivalent to `iex "& {$(irm get.scoop.sh)} -RunAsAdmin"`.
62	        $installer = [scriptblock]::Create((Invoke-RestMethod get.scoop.sh))
63	        if (Test-Admin) {
64	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (elevated console: passing -RunAsAdmin)'
65	            & $installer -RunAsAdmin
66	        } else {
67	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (user-scoped, no admin)'
68	            & $installer
69	        }
70	    }
71	    foreach ($b in 'main', 'extras', 'versions', 'nerd-fonts') {
72	        scoop bucket add $b *> $null
73	    }
74	}
75	
76	function Get-ScoopInstalled {
77	    try { return @((scoop export | ConvertFrom-Json).apps.name) } catch { return @() }
78	}
79	
80	function Scoop-Install([string[]]$apps) {
81	    if (-not $apps) { return }
82	    # Wrap in @() so a lone survivor stays an array. Without it PowerShell
83	    # unwraps a single match to a bare string, and `scoop install @missing`
84	    # then splats it one CHARACTER per arg => "Couldn't find manifest for 'm'"
85	    # when only "mise" was missing.
86	    $missing = @($apps | Where-Object { ($_ -split '/')[-1] -notin (Get-ScoopInstalled) })
87	    if (-not $missing) { Info 'scoop: all requested tools already present'; return }
88	    Info "scoop install: $($missing -join ', ')"
89	    try { scoop install @missing } catch { Write-Warning "scoop batch error: $_" }
90	    # Anything still absent after the batch = failed (scoop keeps going past a bad one).
91	    $after = Get-ScoopInstalled
92	    foreach ($app in $missing) {
93	        $name = ($app -split '/')[-1]
94	        if ($name -notin $after) { Register-Failure "scoop:$name" }
95	    }
96	}

99	function Winget-Install([string]$id) {
100	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "winget:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
101	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
102	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
103	    Info "winget install $id"
104	    winget install --id $id -e --silent --scope user `
105	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
106	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
107	        # some packages don't support --scope user; retry machine scope.
108	        winget install --id $id -e --silent `
109	            --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
110	    }
111	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "winget:$id" }
112	}

368	# -- like herdr -- we fetch them from upstream into the Clink profile dir at apply
369	# time. Network/offline failures are collected, never fatal; starship still works.
370	Scoop-Install @('clink')
371	if (Have clink) {
372	    clink autorun install *> $null                 # HKCU AutoRun; inject into every cmd.exe (no admin)
373	    $clinkDir = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'clink'
374	    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $clinkDir | Out-Null
375	    $clinkPlugins = @{
376	        'zoxide.lua' = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shunsambongi/clink-zoxide/master/zoxide.lua'
377	        'fzf.lua'    = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chrisant996/clink-fzf/master/fzf.lua'
378	    }
379	    foreach ($name in $clinkPlugins.Keys) {
380	        Info "clink plugin: $name"
381	        try { Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Uri $clinkPlugins[$name] -OutFile (Join-Path $clinkDir $name) }
382	        catch { Register-Failure "clink-plugin:$name" }
383	    }
384	    # fzf.default_bindings is a setting DEFINED INSIDE fzf.lua, and `clink set`
385	    # reloads the profile scripts first -- so this must run AFTER fzf.lua is
386	    # downloaded above, else the setting is unknown and the value is silently
387	    # not persisted (the *> $null swallows the "unknown setting" error).
388	    clink set fzf.default_bindings true *> $null    # Ctrl-R history / Ctrl-T files / Alt-C dir
389	} else { Register-Failure 'clink (autorun/plugins not configured)' }
390	{{ end -}}
391	
392	{{ if .installTry -}}
393	# try (tobi/try) — Ruby ephemeral-workspace CLI (gem try-cli). Ruby may already
394	# be present via installExtraRuntimes; install it regardless so installTry is
395	# self-contained (Scoop-Install no-ops if ruby is already there). The pwsh
396	# integration lives in profile.d/32_try.ps1 (command: `tri`).
397	Scoop-Install @('ruby')
398	if (Have gem) {
399	    Info 'gem install try-cli'
400	{{- if .useChineseMirror }}
401	    gem install --clear-sources --source https://gems.ruby-china.com try-cli 2>&1 | Out-Host
402	{{- else }}
403	    gem install try-cli 2>&1 | Out-Host
404	{{- end }}
405	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'gem:try-cli' }
406	} else { Register-Failure 'gem:try-cli (ruby/gem unavailable)' }
407	{{ end -}}
408	
409	if ($script:Failures.Count -gt 0) {
410	    Write-Warning ("package install: {0} item(s) failed and were skipped:" -f $script:Failures.Count)
411	    $script:Failures | ForEach-Object { Write-Warning "  - $_" }
412	    Write-Warning "Re-run 'just apply' (or 'chezmoi apply') to retry, or install them manually."
413	} else {
414	    Info 'package install complete — no failures'
415	}
416

# Tools

The install set lives in `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`
(the single source of truth), gated by the init toggles. **scoop** handles CLI
tools; **winget** handles GUI apps.

## CLI tools (scoop)

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| git, gh, glab | version control + GitHub / GitLab CLIs |
| neovim | editor (LazyVim, pre-configured) |
| lazygit | git TUI (delta pager configured) |
| zoxide | smarter `cd` |
| fzf, fd, ripgrep | fuzzy find / file search / grep |
| bat, eza | `cat` / `ls` replacements |
| delta | git diff pager |
| jq | JSON processor |
| yazi, btop | file manager / system monitor |
| television (`tv`) | fuzzy picker / channel launcher |
| tldr (tlrc) | community man-page cheatsheets; `tldrf` adds `zh_TW → zh → en` fallback |
| gh dash | GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (gh extension; installs once `gh` is authenticated) |
| starship | prompt |
| node | JS runtime (`nodejs-lts`) |
| uv | Python package/runtime manager |
| bun | JS runtime + package manager (backs copilot-proxy) |
| just | task runner (this repo's `justfile`) |
| make | GNU make |
| gcc | C compiler for Neovim tree-sitter parsers (MinGW-w64; nvim-treesitter's `main` branch compiles via `tree-sitter build` → Rust `cc` crate, which needs gcc/clang/MSVC — **not** zig) |
| tree-sitter | tree-sitter CLI (nvim-treesitter `main` branch) |
| win32yank | clipboard provider for Neovim |
| gnupg | `gpg` (git commit signing, verification) |

Runtimes are **native (scoop), not mise** — see [rationale](rationale.md#runtimes-scoop-natives-not-mise-on-windows).
`node` (`nodejs-lts`) and `bun` come from scoop; `go`, `rust` (rustup), `ruby`
are added when **Extra runtimes** is enabled. A default **Python** is uv-managed:
`uv python install --default --preview` puts `python`/`python3` in `~/.local/bin`
(on PATH ahead of the Store's `python.exe` app-alias), so `python`, `uv run`, and
`uv venv` all work without the Microsoft Store.

## GUI apps (winget)

| App | winget id |
|---|---|
| VSCode | `Microsoft.VisualStudioCode` |
| Cursor | `Anysphere.Cursor` |
| Windows Terminal | `Microsoft.WindowsTerminal` |
| Alacritty | `Alacritty.Alacritty` |
| WezTerm | `wez.wezterm` (tmux-like multiplexer; managed `~/.config/wezterm/wezterm.lua` sets pwsh as the shell + Nerd Font) |
| PowerToys | `Microsoft.PowerToys` |
| Raycast | `9PFXXSHC64H3` (msstore) |
| Antigravity | `Google.Antigravity` |
| Docker Desktop | `Docker.DockerDesktop` (WSL2 backend via the `installWsl` toggle — see below) |
| Discord | `Discord.Discord` |
| Claude Desktop | `Anthropic.Claude` |
| ChatGPT | `9PLM9XGG6VKS` (msstore) |
| Chrome | `Google.Chrome` |
| Arc | `TheBrowserCompany.Arc` |
| Zen Browser | `Zen-Team.Zen-Browser` |
| Grammarly | `Grammarly.Grammarly` |

**How should the apprise install + Claude notify hooks be gated? (The install would be `uv tool install --with pywin32 apprise`, and the hooks go into settings-overlay.json.)**

Options:
- **Piggyback on installCodingAgents** - Install apprise + wire notify hooks whenever coding agents are enabled — matches the unix repo (notifications on by default with Claude Code). No new init prompt, no CI/doc-flag upkeep. Downside: no clean per-user opt-out (removing the hook gets re-added on next apply).

**Which Claude Code events should pop a Windows toast? (windows:// via apprise)**

Options:
- **Notification + Stop** - Full parity with the unix notify.sh — toast when Claude needs attention (permission prompt / idle) AND when a task finishes. Note: Stop fires on every turn end, so this is chattier.
- **Notification only** - Toast only when Claude needs your attention (permission prompt / idle waiting). Quieter — no toast on every task completion.


**Answer:** Piggyback on installCodingAgents, Notification + Stop

```md
# Plan: apprise `windows://` desktop toasts for Claude Code

## Context

The parent (macOS/Linux) dotfiles repo wires Claude Code desktop notifications
through a single clean pattern: an OS-agnostic hook script (`notify.sh`) that
just calls `apprise --tag desktop`, with all platform routing delegated to a
chezmoi-templated `~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml`. That template **already has
a `windows://` branch** — the Windows work was 90% designed there but never
shipped in this Windows-only repo, which today has **no notification mechanism
at all** and an overlay with **no `hooks` key**.

This change ports that pattern natively to Windows: install `apprise` (with the
`pywin32` backend `windows://` needs), ship an apprise config selecting the
native Windows toast backend, add a **native-pwsh** `notify.ps1` hook, and wire
the Claude `Notification` + `Stop` events to it via `claude/settings-overlay.json`
— mirroring the parent's `dot_claude/modify_settings.json`. Because we're now
adding hooks, the overlay merger is upgraded to the parent's **additive,
hook-aware** merge so it never clobbers hook entries other tools/the user add.

**Decisions (confirmed with user):**
- **Gating:** piggyback on `installCodingAgents` (matches parent's on-by-default
  behavior; no new init prompt → no invariant-#1 CI/doc-flag upkeep).
- **Events:** `Notification` **and** `Stop` (full parity with parent `notify.sh`).

`windows://` facts (appriseit.com/services/windows): needs `pywin32`; URL is bare
`windows://` (`?duration=N` optional, default 12s); body capped ~250 chars;
local-desktop only (exactly our use case).

## Changes

### 1. Install apprise — `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`

Inside the existing `{{ "{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}" }}` block (currently
ends after `Scoop-Install @('extras/opencode-desktop')`, ~line 269), add — using
the exact `uv tool install` + `Have uv` + `Register-Failure` precedent that
LiteLLM uses at line 337:

\```powershell
# apprise -> Windows toast notifications for the Claude Code notify hook.
# windows:// needs pywin32 (win32api/win32con/win32gui); pull it into the tool venv.
if (Have uv) {
    Info 'uv tool install apprise (+pywin32 for windows://)'
    uv tool install --with pywin32 apprise
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:apprise' }
}
\```

### 2. apprise config — new `dot_config/apprise/` (chezmoi → `~/.config/apprise/`)

`dot_config/` already deploys to `~/.config` (starship/yazi/herdr). Windows-only
repo, so no per-OS templating — just the `windows://` branch.

**`dot_config/apprise/apprise.yaml`** (new, plain — no template directives):
\```yaml
version: 1
# Managed by chezmoi. Desktop toasts on Windows via the native windows://
# backend. The Claude notify hook (~/.claude/hooks/notify.ps1) targets this by
# the `desktop` tag. Add remote services (Discord/Slack/...) in custom.yaml,
# which is created once and never overwritten.
include:
  - custom.yaml
urls:
  - windows://:
      tag: desktop
\```

**`dot_config/apprise/create_custom.yaml`** (new; `create_` = write once, never
overwrite — keeps user-added service URLs w/ secrets out of chezmoi's hands,
matching the parent repo):
\```yaml
# Your custom Apprise services (created once; never overwritten by chezmoi).
#   urls:
#     - discord://webhook_id/webhook_token:
#         tag: chat
version: 1
urls: []
\```

**`dot_config/apprise/example.yaml`** (new, optional, tracked reference) — a few
commented Discord/Slack/mailto examples + link to appriseit.com config docs
(mirror parent's `example.yaml`). Skip if trimming scope.

### 3. Native-pwsh hook — new `dot_claude/hooks/notify.ps1` (→ `~/.claude/hooks/notify.ps1`)

`dot_claude/` already deploys (the skill renderer). Native pwsh, not a ported
bash `notify.sh` (repo philosophy + avoids a jq dependency). Reads the hook JSON
from stdin, builds title/body, fires apprise. Best-effort, always `exit 0`.

\```powershell
#Requires -Version 7
# Claude Code notification hook -> Windows toast via apprise (windows://).
# Native-pwsh port of the parent repo's dot_claude/hooks/notify.sh. Config lives
# in ~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml (tag: desktop). Never throws; always exit 0.
$ErrorActionPreference = 'SilentlyContinue'

# Hooks launch pwsh with -NoProfile, so ~/.local/bin (uv tool bin, has apprise)
# isn't on PATH from 00_env.ps1 — add it here.
$localBin = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
if ((Test-Path $localBin -PathType Container) -and ($env:PATH -notlike "*$localBin*")) {
    $env:PATH = "$localBin;$env:PATH"
}

$raw = [Console]::In.ReadToEnd()
try { $p = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { $p = $null }

$event = if ($p.hook_event_name) { $p.hook_event_name } else { 'unknown' }
$title = if ($p.title) { $p.title } else { 'Claude Code' }
$msg   = "$($p.message)"
$ntype = "$($p.notification_type)"

# Stop carries no title/message — synthesize a completion toast (matches notify.sh).
if ($event -eq 'Stop') { $title = 'Claude Code'; $msg = 'Task finished' }

$fullTitle = if ($ntype) { "$title [$ntype]" } else { $title }
if ($msg.Length -gt 250) { $msg = $msg.Substring(0, 247) + '...' }  # windows:// body cap

if (Get-Command apprise -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
    $cfg = Join-Path $HOME '.config\apprise\apprise.yaml'
    if (Test-Path $cfg) { apprise --config $cfg --tag desktop --title $fullTitle --body $msg *> $null }
    else                { apprise --title $fullTitle --body $msg 'windows://' *> $null }  # fallback
}
exit 0
\```

### 4. Wire the hooks — `claude/settings-overlay.json`

Add a `hooks` key. Command mirrors the proven `statusLine` style in this same
file (POSIX-sh, `${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}`, `exec`) — these command
strings run under Claude's cygwin/msys bash on Windows (cf. commit f7a73eb), and
msys path-mangles the arg to a Windows path for `pwsh.exe`, same as the existing
statusLine does for `node`:

\```json
"hooks": {
  "Notification": [
    { "matcher": "", "hooks": [
      { "type": "command", "command": "exec pwsh -NoProfile -File \"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/hooks/notify.ps1\"" }
    ] }
  ],
  "Stop": [
    { "hooks": [
      { "type": "command", "command": "exec pwsh -NoProfile -File \"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/hooks/notify.ps1\"" }
    ] }
  ]
}
\```

### 5. Make the merger hook-aware/additive — `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl`

Today `Merge-Hashtable` **replaces arrays wholesale** (deliberately deferred:
"the current overlay has no `hooks` key… if you add hooks later, revisit"). We're
adding hooks now → port the parent's jq additive merge from
`dot_claude/modify_settings.json`: recurse into the `hooks` object, and for each
event **append only overlay entries whose `.hooks[0].command` isn't already
present**, preserving live entries. Add a `hooks` special-case to
`Merge-Hashtable` plus a helper:

\```powershell
function Merge-Hashtable([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
    foreach ($k in $overlay.Keys) {
        if ($k -eq 'hooks' -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
            if ($base[$k] -isnot [hashtable]) { $base[$k] = @{} }
            Merge-ClaudeHooks $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
        }
        elseif ($base.ContainsKey($k) -and ($base[$k] -is [hashtable]) -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
            Merge-Hashtable $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
        }
        else { $base[$k] = $overlay[$k] }
    }
}

# Additive per-event merge (mirrors modify_settings.json's jq reduce): append
# overlay entries whose .hooks[0].command isn't already live. Wholesale-replacing
# the arrays would clobber entries the user / other tools add at runtime.
function Merge-ClaudeHooks([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
    foreach ($event in $overlay.Keys) {
        $live = if ($null -ne $base[$event]) { @($base[$event]) } else { @() }
        $liveCmds = @($live | ForEach-Object { $_.hooks[0].command })
        $additions = @($overlay[$event] | Where-Object {
            $cmd = $_.hooks[0].command
            (-not $cmd) -or ($liveCmds -notcontains $cmd)
        })
        $base[$event] = @($live + $additions)
    }
}
\```

Update the script's header comment (it currently claims the parent's jq merge was
*not* ported) to note the additive hook merge is now in place.

### 6. Docs / source-of-truth mirrors (same commit)

- **`docs/tools.md` + `docs/tools.zh-TW.md`** — add apprise (Python tool via uv,
  installed with coding agents) and a one-line note that Claude Code fires
  `windows://` desktop toasts on Notification/Stop via `~/.claude/hooks/notify.ps1`.
- **`CLAUDE.md`** (Architecture → "Config surfaces") — extend the Claude Code
  bullet: the overlay now also wires `Notification`/`Stop` hooks → apprise toast,
  and the merger is additive/hook-aware; add `apprise` (`dot_config/apprise/`,
  `~/.config/apprise/`) to the config-surface list.
- No new init prompt ⇒ **no** `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` / `windows.yml` / skill
  "what's enabled" changes (the point of piggybacking).

## Critical files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` | add `uv tool install --with pywin32 apprise` in the `installCodingAgents` block |

```md
# Plan: apprise `windows://` desktop toasts for Claude Code

## Context

The parent (macOS/Linux) dotfiles repo wires Claude Code desktop notifications
through a single clean pattern: an OS-agnostic hook script (`notify.sh`) that
just calls `apprise --tag desktop`, with all platform routing delegated to a
chezmoi-templated `~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml`. That template **already has
a `windows://` branch** — the Windows work was 90% designed there but never
shipped in this Windows-only repo, which today has **no notification mechanism
at all** and an overlay with **no `hooks` key**.

This change ports that pattern natively to Windows: install `apprise` (with the
`pywin32` backend `windows://` needs), ship an apprise config selecting the
native Windows toast backend, add a **native-pwsh** `notify.ps1` hook, and wire
the Claude `Notification` + `Stop` events to it via `claude/settings-overlay.json`
— mirroring the parent's `dot_claude/modify_settings.json`. Because we're now
adding hooks, the overlay merger is upgraded to the parent's **additive,
hook-aware** merge so it never clobbers hook entries other tools/the user add.

**Decisions (confirmed with user):**
- **Gating:** piggyback on `installCodingAgents` (matches parent's on-by-default
  behavior; no new init prompt → no invariant-#1 CI/doc-flag upkeep).
- **Events:** `Notification` **and** `Stop` (full parity with parent `notify.sh`).

`windows://` facts (appriseit.com/services/windows): needs `pywin32`; URL is bare
`windows://` (`?duration=N` optional, default 12s); body capped ~250 chars;
local-desktop only (exactly our use case).

## Changes

### 1. Install apprise — `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`

Inside the existing `{{ "{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}" }}` block (currently
ends after `Scoop-Install @('extras/opencode-desktop')`, ~line 269), add — using
the exact `uv tool install` + `Have uv` + `Register-Failure` precedent that
LiteLLM uses at line 337:

\```powershell
# apprise -> Windows toast notifications for the Claude Code notify hook.
# windows:// needs pywin32 (win32api/win32con/win32gui); pull it into the tool venv.
if (Have uv) {
    Info 'uv tool install apprise (+pywin32 for windows://)'
    uv tool install --with pywin32 apprise
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:apprise' }
}
\```

### 2. apprise config — new `dot_config/apprise/` (chezmoi → `~/.config/apprise/`)

`dot_config/` already deploys to `~/.config` (starship/yazi/herdr). Windows-only
repo, so no per-OS templating — just the `windows://` branch.

**`dot_config/apprise/apprise.yaml`** (new, plain — no template directives):
\```yaml
version: 1
# Managed by chezmoi. Desktop toasts on Windows via the native windows://
# backend. The Claude notify hook (~/.claude/hooks/notify.ps1) targets this by
# the `desktop` tag. Add remote services (Discord/Slack/...) in custom.yaml,
# which is created once and never overwritten.
include:
  - custom.yaml
urls:
  - windows://:
      tag: desktop
\```

**`dot_config/apprise/create_custom.yaml`** (new; `create_` = write once, never
overwrite — keeps user-added service URLs w/ secrets out of chezmoi's hands,
matching the parent repo):
\```yaml
# Your custom Apprise services (created once; never overwritten by chezmoi).
#   urls:
#     - discord://webhook_id/webhook_token:
#         tag: chat
version: 1
urls: []
\```

**`dot_config/apprise/example.yaml`** (new, optional, tracked reference) — a few
commented Discord/Slack/mailto examples + link to appriseit.com config docs
(mirror parent's `example.yaml`). Skip if trimming scope.

### 3. Native-pwsh hook — new `dot_claude/hooks/notify.ps1` (→ `~/.claude/hooks/notify.ps1`)

`dot_claude/` already deploys (the skill renderer). Native pwsh, not a ported
bash `notify.sh` (repo philosophy + avoids a jq dependency). Reads the hook JSON
from stdin, builds title/body, fires apprise. Best-effort, always `exit 0`.

\```powershell
#Requires -Version 7
# Claude Code notification hook -> Windows toast via apprise (windows://).
# Native-pwsh port of the parent repo's dot_claude/hooks/notify.sh. Config lives
# in ~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml (tag: desktop). Never throws; always exit 0.
$ErrorActionPreference = 'SilentlyContinue'

# Hooks launch pwsh with -NoProfile, so ~/.local/bin (uv tool bin, has apprise)
# isn't on PATH from 00_env.ps1 — add it here.
$localBin = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
if ((Test-Path $localBin -PathType Container) -and ($env:PATH -notlike "*$localBin*")) {
    $env:PATH = "$localBin;$env:PATH"
}

$raw = [Console]::In.ReadToEnd()
try { $p = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { $p = $null }

$event = if ($p.hook_event_name) { $p.hook_event_name } else { 'unknown' }
$title = if ($p.title) { $p.title } else { 'Claude Code' }
$msg   = "$($p.message)"
$ntype = "$($p.notification_type)"

# Stop carries no title/message — synthesize a completion toast (matches notify.sh).
if ($event -eq 'Stop') { $title = 'Claude Code'; $msg = 'Task finished' }

$fullTitle = if ($ntype) { "$title [$ntype]" } else { $title }
if ($msg.Length -gt 250) { $msg = $msg.Substring(0, 247) + '...' }  # windows:// body cap

if (Get-Command apprise -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
    $cfg = Join-Path $HOME '.config\apprise\apprise.yaml'
    if (Test-Path $cfg) { apprise --config $cfg --tag desktop --title $fullTitle --body $msg *> $null }
    else                { apprise --title $fullTitle --body $msg 'windows://' *> $null }  # fallback
}
exit 0
\```

### 4. Wire the hooks — `claude/settings-overlay.json`

Add a `hooks` key. Command mirrors the proven `statusLine` style in this same
file (POSIX-sh, `${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}`, `exec`) — these command
strings run under Claude's cygwin/msys bash on Windows (cf. commit f7a73eb), and
msys path-mangles the arg to a Windows path for `pwsh.exe`, same as the existing
statusLine does for `node`:

\```json
"hooks": {
  "Notification": [
    { "matcher": "", "hooks": [
      { "type": "command", "command": "exec pwsh -NoProfile -File \"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/hooks/notify.ps1\"" }
    ] }
  ],
  "Stop": [
    { "hooks": [
      { "type": "command", "command": "exec pwsh -NoProfile -File \"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/hooks/notify.ps1\"" }
    ] }
  ]
}
\```

### 5. Make the merger hook-aware/additive — `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl`

Today `Merge-Hashtable` **replaces arrays wholesale** (deliberately deferred:
"the current overlay has no `hooks` key… if you add hooks later, revisit"). We're
adding hooks now → port the parent's jq additive merge from
`dot_claude/modify_settings.json`: recurse into the `hooks` object, and for each
event **append only overlay entries whose `.hooks[0].command` isn't already
present**, preserving live entries. Add a `hooks` special-case to
`Merge-Hashtable` plus a helper:

\```powershell
function Merge-Hashtable([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
    foreach ($k in $overlay.Keys) {
        if ($k -eq 'hooks' -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
            if ($base[$k] -isnot [hashtable]) { $base[$k] = @{} }
            Merge-ClaudeHooks $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
        }
        elseif ($base.ContainsKey($k) -and ($base[$k] -is [hashtable]) -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
            Merge-Hashtable $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
        }
        else { $base[$k] = $overlay[$k] }
    }
}

# Additive per-event merge (mirrors modify_settings.json's jq reduce): append
# overlay entries whose .hooks[0].command isn't already live. Wholesale-replacing
# the arrays would clobber entries the user / other tools add at runtime.
function Merge-ClaudeHooks([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
    foreach ($event in $overlay.Keys) {
        $live = if ($null -ne $base[$event]) { @($base[$event]) } else { @() }
        $liveCmds = @($live | ForEach-Object { $_.hooks[0].command })
        $additions = @($overlay[$event] | Where-Object {
            $cmd = $_.hooks[0].command
            (-not $cmd) -or ($liveCmds -notcontains $cmd)
        })
        $base[$event] = @($live + $additions)
    }
}
\```

Update the script's header comment (it currently claims the parent's jq merge was
*not* ported) to note the additive hook merge is now in place.

### 6. Docs / source-of-truth mirrors (same commit)

- **`docs/tools.md` + `docs/tools.zh-TW.md`** — add apprise (Python tool via uv,
  installed with coding agents) and a one-line note that Claude Code fires
  `windows://` desktop toasts on Notification/Stop via `~/.claude/hooks/notify.ps1`.
- **`CLAUDE.md`** (Architecture → "Config surfaces") — extend the Claude Code
  bullet: the overlay now also wires `Notification`/`Stop` hooks → apprise toast,
  and the merger is additive/hook-aware; add `apprise` (`dot_config/apprise/`,
  `~/.config/apprise/`) to the config-surface list.
- No new init prompt ⇒ **no** `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` / `windows.yml` / skill
  "what's enabled" changes (the point of piggybacking).

## Critical files

| `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` | add `uv tool install --with pywin32 apprise` in the `installCodingAgents` block |

#Requires -Version 7
# Claude Code notification hook -> Windows toast via apprise (windows://).
# Native-pwsh port of the parent repo's dot_claude/hooks/notify.sh. Config lives
# in ~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml (tag: desktop). Never throws; always exit 0.

# isn't on PATH from 00_env.ps1 — add it here.
$localBin = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
if ((Test-Path $localBin -PathType Container) -and ($env:PATH -notlike "*$localBin*")) {
    $env:PATH = "$localBin;$env:PATH"
}

$raw = [Console]::In.ReadToEnd()
try { $p = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { $p = $null }

$event = if ($p.hook_event_name) { $p.hook_event_name } else { 'unknown' }
$title = if ($p.title) { $p.title } else { 'Claude Code' }
$msg   = "$($p.message)"
$ntype = "$($p.notification_type)"

# Stop carries no title/message — synthesize a completion toast (matches notify.sh).
if ($event -eq 'Stop') { $title = 'Claude Code'; $msg = 'Task finished' }

$fullTitle = if ($ntype) { "$title [$ntype]" } else { $title }
if ($msg.Length -gt 250) { $msg = $msg.Substring(0, 247) + '...' }  # windows:// body cap

if (Get-Command apprise -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
    $cfg = Join-Path $HOME '.config\apprise\apprise.yaml'
    if (Test-Path $cfg) { apprise --config $cfg --tag desktop --title $fullTitle --body $msg *> $null }
    else                { apprise --title $fullTitle --body $msg 'windows://' *> $null }  # fallback
}
exit 0
```

### 4. Wire the hooks — `claude/settings-overlay.json`

Add a `hooks` key. Command mirrors the proven `statusLine` style in this same
file (POSIX-sh, `${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}`, `exec`) — these command
strings run under Claude's cygwin/msys bash on Windows (cf. commit f7a73eb), and
msys path-mangles the arg to a Windows path for `pwsh.exe`, same as the existing
statusLine does for `node`:

```json
"hooks": {
  "Notification": [
    { "matcher": "", "hooks": [
      { "type": "command", "command": "exec pwsh -NoProfile -File \"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/hooks/notify.ps1\"" }
    ] }
  ],
  "Stop": [
    { "hooks": [
      { "type": "command", "command": "exec pwsh -NoProfile -File \"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/hooks/notify.ps1\"" }
    ] }
  ]
}
```

### 5. Make the merger hook-aware/additive — `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl`

Today `Merge-Hashtable` **replaces arrays wholesale** (deliberately deferred:
"the current overlay has no `hooks` key… if you add hooks later, revisit"). We're
adding hooks now → port the parent's jq additive merge from
`dot_claude/modify_settings.json`: recurse into the `hooks` object, and for each
event **append only overlay entries whose `.hooks[0].command` isn't already
present**, preserving live entries. Add a `hooks` special-case to
`Merge-Hashtable` plus a helper:

```powershell
function Merge-Hashtable([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
    foreach ($k in $overlay.Keys) {
        if ($k -eq 'hooks' -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
            if ($base[$k] -isnot [hashtable]) { $base[$k] = @{} }
            Merge-ClaudeHooks $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
        }
        elseif ($base.ContainsKey($k) -and ($base[$k] -is [hashtable]) -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
            Merge-Hashtable $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
        }
        else { $base[$k] = $overlay[$k] }
    }
}

# Additive per-event merge (mirrors modify_settings.json's jq reduce): append
# overlay entries whose .hooks[0].command isn't already live. Wholesale-replacing
# the arrays would clobber entries the user / other tools add at runtime.
function Merge-ClaudeHooks([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
    foreach ($event in $overlay.Keys) {
        $live = if ($null -ne $base[$event]) { @($base[$event]) } else { @() }
        $liveCmds = @($live | ForEach-Object { $_.hooks[0].command })
        $additions = @($overlay[$event] | Where-Object {
            $cmd = $_.hooks[0].command
            (-not $cmd) -or ($liveCmds -notcontains $cmd)
        })
        $base[$event] = @($live + $additions)
    }
}
```

Update the script's header comment (it currently claims the parent's jq merge was
*not* ported) to note the additive hook merge is now in place.

- **`docs/tools.md` + `docs/tools.zh-TW.md`** — add apprise (Python tool via uv,
  installed with coding agents) and a one-line note that Claude Code fires
  `windows://` desktop toasts on Notification/Stop via `~/.claude/hooks/notify.ps1`.
- **`CLAUDE.md`** (Architecture → "Config surfaces") — extend the Claude Code
  bullet: the overlay now also wires `Notification`/`Stop` hooks → apprise toast,
  and the merger is additive/hook-aware; add `apprise` (`dot_config/apprise/`,
  `~/.config/apprise/`) to the config-surface list.
- No new init prompt ⇒ **no** `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` / `windows.yml` / skill
  "what's enabled" changes (the point of piggybacking).

## Critical files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` | add `uv tool install --with pywin32 apprise` in the `installCodingAgents` block |
| `dot_config/apprise/apprise.yaml` | **new** — `windows://` + `tag: desktop`, `include: custom.yaml` |
| `dot_config/apprise/create_custom.yaml` | **new** — create-once user services |
| `dot_config/apprise/example.yaml` | **new (optional)** — reference examples |
| `dot_claude/hooks/notify.ps1` | **new** — native-pwsh stdin→apprise hook |
| `claude/settings-overlay.json` | add `hooks` (Notification + Stop) |
| `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl` | additive hook-aware merge |
| `docs/tools.md`, `docs/tools.zh-TW.md`, `CLAUDE.md` | doc mirrors |

## Verification

Off-Windows (this dev box) — the CLAUDE.md isolated-apply + render/parse idiom:

1. **JSON validity:** `claude/settings-overlay.json` parses (`python -c "import json,sys;json.load(open('claude/settings-overlay.json'))"`); the two YAML files parse (`python -c "import yaml;yaml.safe_load(open(...))"` if PyYAML available, else eyeball).
2. **Render + pwsh-parse** the two edited `.ps1.tmpl` files via `chezmoi execute-template` (with every prompt supplied, per CLAUDE.md), then `pwsh -NoProfile -c "[scriptblock]::Create((Get-Content -Raw r.ps1)) > $null"` to confirm they parse.
3. **Merge behavior (core risk):** with a local pwsh, dot-run the rendered merger against a synthetic `settings.json` and assert:
   - fresh file (no `hooks`) → gets both events, each a JSON **array** (guard the pwsh single-element-array serialization quirk: re-parse output, assert `hooks.Stop` is a list of len 1);
   - re-run is idempotent (no duplicate entry — command already present);
   - a pre-existing user `Notification` entry is **preserved** and ours appended.
4. **PSScriptAnalyzer** clean on `notify.ps1` + edited scripts (`Invoke-ScriptAnalyzer -Settings ./PSScriptAnalyzerSettings.psd1`).
5. `just docs-build` stays green (`--strict`).

On a real Windows box (the `windows-latest` CI is the true gate):
6. After apply: `apprise --config ~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml --tag desktop --title Test --body Hello` pops a toast (proves pywin32/`windows://` works).
7. Trigger a Claude `Stop` (finish any turn) and a `Notification` (e.g. a permission prompt) → confirm a "Claude Code / Task finished" toast and an attention toast appear.

## Risks & notes

- **pwsh single-element array JSON** — the one behavior to verify (step 3);
  PS7 should keep nested `[object[]]` as arrays, but Claude requires `hooks.<event>`
  to be an array. If it collapses, write a `pitfalls/` doc + force-wrap.
- **pywin32 in a uv tool venv** — `--with pywin32` should suffice (apprise imports
  `win32*` directly; no `pywin32_postinstall` needed for toast use). If a real box
  proves otherwise, `pitfalls/` it. `uv:apprise` failure is non-fatal (Register-Failure).
- **`~/.local/bin` on PATH** — handled inside `notify.ps1` (hooks run `-NoProfile`).
- **No clean opt-out** (accepted tradeoff of piggybacking): removing the hook by
  hand gets re-added on next apply. A future `installApprise` toggle could add one.
```

1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1 — merge a managed overlay into
3	# ~/.claude/settings.json so claude-hud (statusline HUD), the pyright-lsp
4	# plugin, and a couple of safe defaults deploy on Windows. Companion to the
5	# parent (macOS/Linux) repo's dot_claude/modify_settings.json; that repo uses a
6	# `modify_` + jq merge, which is unreliable on Windows (invariant #5), so we use
7	# the same run_onchange pwsh-merger idiom as the editor overlays instead.
8	#
9	# Why a merge (not a managed dot_claude/settings.json)? Claude Code rewrites
10	# this file at runtime (plugins it installs, permissions the user grants). A
11	# static managed file would clobber all of that on every apply. We instead
12	# recursively deep-merge only our keys in, preserving every live key.
13	#
14	# Gated on installCodingAgents — no Claude Code, nothing to configure. Re-runs
15	# when the embedded overlay (claude/settings-overlay.json) changes.
16	{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}
17	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'   # best-effort; never abort the apply
18	
19	# Embedded at render time from the (chezmoi-ignored) claude/ source, so this
20	# script is self-contained and re-fires when the overlay changes.
21	$overlayJson = @'
22	{{ include "claude/settings-overlay.json" }}
23	'@
24	
25	$overlay = $overlayJson | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable
26	
27	# Recursive deep-merge: overlay leaves win; live-only keys are preserved. Unlike
28	# the editor overlay's shallow top-level assign, this keeps the user's nested
29	# state (e.g. permissions.allow/deny arrays, other enabledPlugins) intact.
30	function Merge-Hashtable([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
31	    foreach ($k in $overlay.Keys) {
32	        if ($base.ContainsKey($k) -and ($base[$k] -is [hashtable]) -and ($overlay[$k] -is [hashtable])) {
33	            Merge-Hashtable $base[$k] $overlay[$k]
34	        } else {
35	            $base[$k] = $overlay[$k]
36	        }
37	    }
38	}
39	
40	# Respect CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (matches the statusLine command + claude-hud setup).
41	$claudeDir = if ($env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) { $env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR } else { Join-Path $HOME '.claude' }
42	$settingsPath = Join-Path $claudeDir 'settings.json'
43	
44	$settings = @{}
45	if (Test-Path $settingsPath) {
46	    $raw = Get-Content -Raw $settingsPath
47	    if ($raw.Trim()) {
48	        try { $settings = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable }
49	        catch { Write-Warning "could not parse $settingsPath; not overwriting"; return }
50	    }
51	}
52	
53	Merge-Hashtable $settings $overlay
54	New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $claudeDir | Out-Null
55	$settings | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 12 | Set-Content -Path $settingsPath -Encoding utf8
56	Write-Host "claude overlay -> $settingsPath"
57	{{ end -}}
58
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```ps1
#Requires -Version 7
# notify.ps1 — Claude Code notification hook -> Windows toast via apprise.
#
# Native-pwsh port of the parent (macOS/Linux) repo's dot_claude/hooks/notify.sh.
# Reads the hook JSON payload from stdin, builds a title/body, and fires
# `apprise --tag desktop`, which resolves to the native windows:// backend
# configured in ~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml. Wired to the Notification and
# Stop events by claude/settings-overlay.json.
#
# Best-effort by design: it must never block or fail a Claude turn, so it
# swallows every error and always exits 0. If apprise isn't installed yet, it
# simply no-ops.
$ErrorActionPreference = 'SilentlyContinue'

# Claude launches hook commands with `pwsh -NoProfile`, so profile.d/00_env.ps1
# hasn't run and ~/.local/bin (the uv tool bin dir holding apprise.exe) may not
# be on PATH. Prepend it defensively.
$localBin = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
if ((Test-Path $localBin -PathType Container) -and ($env:PATH -notlike "*$localBin*")) {
    $env:PATH = "$localBin;$env:PATH"
}

$raw = [Console]::In.ReadToEnd()
try { $payload = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { $payload = $null }

$event = if ($payload.hook_event_name) { $payload.hook_event_name } else { 'unknown' }
$title = if ($payload.title) { $payload.title } else { 'Claude Code' }
$msg   = "$($payload.message)"
$ntype = "$($payload.notification_type)"

# The Stop event carries no title/message — synthesize a completion toast
# (matches the parent notify.sh behavior).
if ($event -eq 'Stop') { $title = 'Claude Code'; $msg = 'Task finished' }

$fullTitle = if ($ntype) { "$title [$ntype]" } else { $title }
# windows:// caps the body around 250 chars; trim so a long message still toasts.
if ($msg.Length -gt 250) { $msg = $msg.Substring(0, 247) + '...' }

if (Get-Command apprise -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
    $cfg = Join-Path $HOME '.config\apprise\apprise.yaml'
    if (Test-Path $cfg) {
        apprise --config $cfg --tag desktop --title $fullTitle --body $msg *> $null
    } else {
        # Config not deployed yet — fall back to an inline windows:// URL.
        apprise --title $fullTitle --body $msg 'windows://' *> $null
    }
}
exit 0

```

```text
135	## AI agents
136	
137	Enabled by **Install coding agents**:
138	
139	- **Claude Code** — `Anthropic.ClaudeCode` via **winget** (the native Windows
140	  build; distinct from `Anthropic.Claude`, the desktop app under GUI apps).
141	  Upgraded with `just upgrade-winget`, not npm.
142	- **OpenCode** (`opencode-ai`), **Codex** (`@openai/codex`), **Copilot CLI**
143	  (`@github/copilot`) — installed globally via **npm** (provided by the scoop `node`).
144	- **OpenCode Desktop** — GUI companion to the `opencode-ai` CLI, via scoop
145	  (`extras/opencode-desktop`).
146	- **claude-hud** — a statusline HUD that renders below the Claude Code prompt.
147	  Enabled in `~/.claude/settings.json` by a `run_onchange` merger
148	  (`.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl`) that
149	  additively deep-merges an overlay, preserving your live settings. The plugin
150	  self-installs from its marketplace on first launch and runs on the scoop
151	  `bun`/`node` runtime; the merger also enables the `pyright-lsp` plugin and sets
152	  `permissions.defaultMode: auto` + `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt`. **Restart
153	  Claude Code** after `chezmoi apply` to see the HUD. See
154	  [running multiple agents](claude-code-agents.md).
155	
156	The official **ChatGPT** desktop app installs via the Microsoft Store (see GUI
157	apps); **Codex** is the `@openai/codex` npm CLI above, not a Store app. SpecStory
158	has no native-Windows CLI package, so it's omitted here.
159	
160	## PowerShell modules (PSGallery)
161	
162	- **PSFzf** — fzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history).
163	- **AudioDeviceCmdlets** — absolute volume + mute for the `sysvol`/`sysmute` helpers.
164	
165	## Opt-in dev stacks
166	
167	Off by default; enable the matching init prompt:
168	
169	| Toggle | Installs |
170	|---|---|
171	| Local LLM tools | Ollama (`Ollama.Ollama`) + LiteLLM (`uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'`) |
172	| Tunnel tools | ngrok, cloudflared (scoop) |
173	| IaC tools | Terraform, OpenTofu (scoop) + Azure CLI & the `azure-devops` extension (`az devops`/`az repos`, winget) |
174	| OpenSSH server | Microsoft OpenSSH Server (sshd): Windows capability + auto-start service + inbound TCP 22 firewall rule, with pwsh as the default shell. Needs admin — set up on an elevated `chezmoi apply`, or run `just enable-sshd` from an elevated pwsh. |
175	| herdr multiplexer | herdr, a native Windows terminal multiplexer (**preview beta**). No scoop/winget manifest — installs via herdr.dev's `irm \| iex` script; config managed at `~/.config/herdr/config.toml` (pwsh as the default shell). See [rationale](rationale.md#terminal-multiplexer-wezterm-stable-default-herdr-native-beta). |
176	| Clink (cmd.exe) | [Clink](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/) (scoop `main`) — Bash-style line editing for `cmd.exe`, so **starship** + **zoxide** + **fzf** work in the DOS prompt. Reuses the shared `starship.toml`; registers a per-user cmd AutoRun, deploys our `starship.lua`, and fetches the community `clink-zoxide` / `clink-fzf` bridges into `%LocalAppData%\clink`. pwsh stays the default — this is an opt-in secondary shell with prompt + nav parity only. See [rationale](rationale.md#powershell-7-default-cmdexe-optional-via-clink) & [Shell](shell.md#cmdexe-via-clink). |
177	| try (ephemeral workspaces) | [`try`](https://github.com/tobi/try) via `gem install try-cli` (ruby installed if absent). Dated `~/src/tries/YYYY-MM-DD-name` trial dirs + a fuzzy selector; `tri <git-url>` clones into one. The pwsh command is **`tri`** (`try` is a reserved keyword — bareword `try` won't parse; `& try` works). See [Shell](shell.md#try). |
178	
179	**China mirrors** routes pip / npm / cargo / go / node package fetches through GFW
180	mirrors (Tsinghua / npmmirror / goproxy.cn / rsproxy), applied both at install time
181	and in the interactive shell (`profile.d/05_mirrors.ps1`).
182

PY

note "4. pwsh parse of rendered scripts + notify.ps1"
pwsh -NoProfile -c "
\$bad=0
foreach(\$f in @('$TMPD/r10.ps1','$TMPD/r25.ps1','dot_claude/hooks/notify.ps1')){
  \$t=\$null;\$e=\$null
  [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile((Resolve-Path \$f),[ref]\$t,[ref]\$e)|Out-Null
  if(\$e -and \$e.Count){ Write-Host ('  PARSE ERRORS: '+\$f); \$e|ForEach-Object{Write-Host ('    '+\$_.Message)}; \$bad=1 }
  else { Write-Host ('  OK parse: '+(Split-Path \$f -Leaf)) }
}
exit \$bad
" || FAIL=1

note "5. merger behavior (fresh / idempotent / preserve)"

import json,sys

assert isinstance(h["Stop"],list) and len(h["Stop"])==1, ("Stop",h.get("Stop"))

print("  fresh: Notification[1], Stop[1], arrays OK")
PY
# 5b idempotent

python3 - "$C1/settings.json" <<'PY' || FAIL=1

d=json.load(open(sys.argv[1])); h=d["hooks"]
assert len(h["Stop"])==1 and len(h["Notification"])==1, ("dup!",h)
print("  idempotent: still Stop[1], Notification[1] (no dup)")
PY
# 5c preserve a pre-existing user hook
C2="$TMPD/claude2"; mkdir -p "$C2"
cat > "$C2/settings.json" <<'JSON'
{ "hooks": { "Notification": [ { "matcher": "", "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "echo user-hook" } ] } ] } }
JSON
CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR="$C2" pwsh -NoProfile -File "$TMPD/r25.ps1" >/dev/null 2>&1
python3 - "$C2/settings.json" <<'PY' || FAIL=1

d=json.load(open(sys.argv[1])); n=d["hooks"]["Notification"]
cmds=[e["hooks"][0]["command"] for e in n]

assert any("notify.ps1" in c for c in cmds), ("ours missing!",cmds)
assert len(n)==2, ("expected 2",cmds)
print("  preserve: user hook kept + ours appended (Notification[2])")
PY

note "6. PSScriptAnalyzer on notify.ps1 (Errors only)"

\$errs = @(\$r | Where-Object Severity -eq 'Error')
\$r | Format-Table -AutoSize | Out-String | ForEach-Object { if(\$_.Trim()){ Write-Host \$_ } }
if(\$errs.Count){ Write-Host ('  ERRORS: '+\$errs.Count); exit 1 } else { Write-Host '  no PSSA Errors' }
" || FAIL=1

echo

rm -rf "$TMPD"

```ps1
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

function Merge-ClaudeHooks([hashtable]$base, [hashtable]$overlay) {
    foreach ($evt in $overlay.Keys) {
        $live = if ($null -ne $base[$evt]) { @($base[$evt]) } else { @() }
        $liveCmds = @($live | ForEach-Object { $_.hooks[0].command })
        Write-Host "  [$evt] live=$($live.Count) liveCmds=[$($liveCmds -join '|')]"
        $additions = @($overlay[$evt] | Where-Object {
            $cmd = $_.hooks[0].command
            Write-Host "    overlay cmd='$cmd' notcontains=$($liveCmds -notcontains $cmd)"
            (-not $cmd) -or ($liveCmds -notcontains $cmd)
        })
        Write-Host "  [$evt] additions=$($additions.Count)"
        $base[$evt] = @($live + $additions)
    }
}

$overlayJson = @'
{ "hooks": {
  "Notification": [ { "matcher": "", "hooks": [ { "type":"command", "command":"exec pwsh -NoProfile -File notify.ps1" } ] } ],
  "Stop": [ { "hooks": [ { "type":"command", "command":"exec pwsh -NoProfile -File notify.ps1" } ] } ]
} }
'@
$overlay = $overlayJson | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable

$seed = @'
{ "hooks": { "Notification": [ { "matcher": "", "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "echo user-hook" } ] } ] } }
'@
$settings = $seed | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable

Merge-ClaudeHooks $settings.hooks $overlay.hooks
Write-Host "after: Notification count = $(@($settings.hooks.Notification).Count)"
$settings.hooks.Notification | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  entry cmd: $($_.hooks[0].command)" }

So when the hashtable gets unwrapped into `$live`, calling `.Count` on it returns the number of keys (2 in this case: matcher and hooks), which explains the "live=2" output. When piping that hashtable through `ForEach-Object`, it treats it as a single object, so `$_.hooks[0].command` correctly extracts 'echo user-hook'. But then trying to add the hashtable to an array fails because they're incompatible types. The real fix is to ensure the array stays wrapped on assignment instead of relying on the `if` statement's unwrapping behavior.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-12 12:40:43Z)**_

Found the root cause. The `$live = if (...) { @(...) } else { @() }` idiom **unwraps** a single-element array back to a scalar hashtable when the `if` result flows through assignment — so `$live` became a hashtable (`.Count`=2 keys), and `hashtable + array` throws. The fix is a plain two-line assignment (direct `$x = @(...)` preserves array-ness). Let me confirm the fix in the debug harness:
